text: Alice's income in 2015 is $100000. She gets one exemption of $2000 for the year 2015 under section 151(c). Alice is not married.
question: Alice's total exemption for 2015 under section 151(a) is equal to $6000.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* =====================================================
   Statutory rules (abbreviated to what is needed here)
   ===================================================== */

% exemption_amount(Amount) – §151(d)(1)
exemption_amount(2000).

/* §151(b) – taxpayer’s own exemption ----------------- */
taxpayer_exemption(_Taxpayer, Amount) :-
    exemption_amount(Amount).

/* §151(b) – spouse exemption (only when conditions met)
   For this case we encode the general rule: if the
   taxpayer is married and the spouse qualifies, grant
   one exemption amount; otherwise 0.                */
spouse_exemption(Taxpayer, Amount) :-
    married(Taxpayer),                     % fact supplied only if true
    spouse_qualifies(Taxpayer),            % dummy predicate, succeeds if asserted
    exemption_amount(Amount), !.
spouse_exemption(_Taxpayer, 0).

/* §151(c) – dependents ------------------------------- */
dependent_exemption(Taxpayer, TotalAmount) :-
    dependents(Taxpayer, N),               % N = number of qualifying dependents
    exemption_amount(Amount),
    TotalAmount is N * Amount.

/* ----------------------------------------------------
   Total exemption allowed under §151(a)
   ---------------------------------------------------- */
total_exemption(Taxpayer, Total) :-
    taxpayer_exemption(Taxpayer, E1),
    spouse_exemption(Taxpayer,   E2),
    dependent_exemption(Taxpayer,E3),
    Total is E1 + E2 + E3.



/* =====================================================
   Facts from the case of Alice
   ===================================================== */

taxpayer(alice).
income(alice, 100000).
% Alice is not married
%  -> no fact 'married(alice).'
% She has one qualifying dependent (§151(c))
dependents(alice, 1).
% No spouse, so spouse_qualifies/1 will fail automatically.



/* =====================================================
   Verification goal
   ===================================================== */

:- (  total_exemption(alice, 6000)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

/* Statutory rules (excerpted and simplified for this problem) */

% exemption_amount(Amount) – §151(d)(1)
exemption_amount(2000).

% personal_exemption(Taxpayer, Amount) – §151(b) (taxpayer’s own exemption)
personal_exemption(Taxpayer, Amount) :-
    exemption_amount(Amount).

% spouse_exemption/2 would go here, but is omitted: Alice is not married.

% dependent_exemption(Taxpayer, Amount) – §151(c)
dependent_exemption(Taxpayer, Amount) :-
    num_dependents(Taxpayer, N),
    exemption_amount(EA),
    Amount is N * EA.

% total_exemption(Taxpayer, Total) – §151(a)
total_exemption(Taxpayer, Total) :-
    personal_exemption(Taxpayer, PE),
    dependent_exemption(Taxpayer, DE),
    Total is PE + DE.


/* Case facts */

taxpayer(alice).
income(alice, 100000).
marital_status(alice, unmarried).
num_dependents(alice, 1).   % Alice has exactly one dependent exemption under §151(c)


/* Verification query: does Alice’s total §151 exemption equal $4,000? */
:- (  total_exemption(alice, 4000)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob have been married since 2 Feb 2015. Bob has no income for 2015. Alice and Bob file their taxes jointly for 2015.
question: Alice can receive an exemption for Bob under section 151(b) for the year 2015.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
% Statutory rules (relevant extracts)

% A taxpayer may claim an additional exemption for a spouse for a given Year
% if ALL of the following are true (section 151(b)):
%   1. The two persons are spouses.
%   2. They did NOT file a joint return for that Year.
%   3. The spouse had no gross income for that Year.
%   4. The spouse was not the dependent of another taxpayer for that Year.

spouse_exemption_allowed(Taxpayer, Spouse, Year) :-
    spouse(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    no_joint_return_filed(Taxpayer, Spouse, Year),
    has_no_gross_income(Spouse, Year),
    \+ dependent_of(Spouse, _, Year).

% Helper predicate: succeeds when no joint return was filed
no_joint_return_filed(A, B, Y) :-
    \+ filed_joint_return(A, B, Y),
    \+ filed_joint_return(B, A, Y).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Facts from the case

spouse(alice, bob).
spouse(bob, alice).

% They have been married since 2015; that suffices for 2015 status
married(alice, bob, 2015).

% Bob had no income in 2015
has_no_gross_income(bob, 2015).

% They filed a joint return for 2015
filed_joint_return(alice, bob, 2015).

% Bob is not a dependent of another taxpayer in 2015
% (left intentionally absent; default is that he is not)

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Verification goal
:- (  spouse_exemption_allowed(alice, bob, 2015)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob have been married since 2 Feb 2015. Charlie counts as Alice's dependent under section 152(c)(1) for 2015.
question: Alice can claim an exemption with Bob as the dependent for 2015 under section 151(c).
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/*****************************************************************
 Statutory rules (minimal fragments needed for this problem)
*****************************************************************/

% A taxpayer may claim an exemption for each of his dependents
can_claim_exemption(Taxpayer, Person, Year) :-
    dependent(Taxpayer, Person, Year).

% A dependent is either a qualifying child or a qualifying relative
dependent(Taxpayer, Person, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Person, Year).
dependent(Taxpayer, Person, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Person, Year).

/* -------------------------------------------------------------
   Qualifying child  (section 152(c)) – only those relationships
   listed are eligible.  Spouse is NOT listed.
----------------------------------------------------------------*/
qualifying_child(Taxpayer, Child, _Year) :-
    relationship_child(Child, Taxpayer),      % proper relationship
    principal_abode_same(_, _),               % details irrelevant here
    age_requirement_met(_, _),
    not(joint_return_with_spouse(Child, _)).  % simplifying placeholder

/* No facts satisfy relationship_child/2 for this case, 
   so qualifying_child will fail. */

/* -------------------------------------------------------------
   Qualifying relative (section 152(d))
   – must satisfy relationship list which EXCLUDES spouse
----------------------------------------------------------------*/
qualifying_relative(Taxpayer, Person, _Year) :-
    relationship_relative(Person, Taxpayer),  % proper relationship
    no_income(Person, _),                     % income test
    not(qualifying_child(_, Person, _)).      % not qualifying child
                                              
/* relationship_relative/2 enumerates the permitted relationships.
   Spouse is NOT among them.                                    */
relationship_relative(Person, Taxpayer) :-
    % child, sibling, parent, in-law, etc. (spouse excluded)
    ( child_of(Person, Taxpayer)
    ; sibling_of(Person, Taxpayer)
    ; parent_of(Person, Taxpayer)
    ; in_law_of(Person, Taxpayer)
    ; household_member(Person, Taxpayer)
    ),
    not(spouse_of(Person, Taxpayer)).   % explicit exclusion of spouse

/*****************************************************************
 Facts supplied by the case
*****************************************************************/

% Alice and Bob have been married since 2 Feb 2015
spouse_of(alice, bob).
spouse_of(bob, alice).

% Charlie is Alice's qualifying child for 2015
qualifying_child(alice, charlie, 2015).

/*****************************************************************
   Miscellaneous predicates referenced in the rules but not
   relevant to deciding the present issue – left undefined so
   that they simply fail when queried.
*****************************************************************/
relationship_child(_,_)        :- fail.
principal_abode_same(_,_)      :- fail.
age_requirement_met(_,_)       :- fail.
joint_return_with_spouse(_,_)  :- fail.
no_income(_,_)                 :- fail.
child_of(_,_)                  :- fail.
sibling_of(_,_)                :- fail.
parent_of(_,_)                 :- fail.
in_law_of(_,_)                 :- fail.
household_member(_,_)          :- fail.

/*****************************************************************
  Verification goal
*****************************************************************/
:- (  can_claim_exemption(alice, bob, 2015)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

% Statutory rules relevant to the exemption amount under §151(d)

% helper: Year in inclusive range
is_between(Year,Low,High) :- Year >= Low, Year =< High.

% §151(d)(5): exemption amount is zero for taxable years 2018-2025
exemption_amount(_,Year,0) :-
    is_between(Year,2018,2025), !.

% §151(d)(2): exemption amount is zero if another taxpayer may claim the individual
exemption_amount(Person,Year,0) :-
    deduction_allowed_to_another(Person,Year), !.

% §151(d)(1): default exemption amount is $2,000
exemption_amount(Person,Year,2000) :-
    \+ is_between(Year,2018,2025),
    \+ deduction_allowed_to_another(Person,Year).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Case facts

year(2015).

% Alice is NOT a dependent of another taxpayer for 2015
% (so no fact `deduction_allowed_to_another(alice,2015).` is asserted)

% Alice is entitled to her own exemption under §151(b)
entitled_to_exemption(alice,2015,section151b).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Verification of the claim:
%   “Alice's exemption amount under §151(d)(1) is equal to $0.”

:- (  exemption_amount(alice,2015,0)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice is entitled to an exemption for herself under section 151(b) for the year 2015. No other taxpayer is entitled to a deduction for Alice in 2015.
question: Alice's exemption amount under section 151(d)(1) is equal to $2000.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules (relevant excerpts only) ---------- */
base_exemption_amount(2000).

/* special rule for 2018-2025: exemption amount is zero              */
exemption_amount(_Taxpayer, Year, 0) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.

/* general rule (151(d)(1) plus phase-out of 151(d)(3))              
   – to compute a positive exemption amount we MUST know:             
       • the taxpayer’s adjusted gross income (AGI)                   
       • the taxpayer’s filing status (to select the proper threshold)
   If either piece of information is missing, the rule cannot apply.  */
exemption_amount(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    Year < 2018,
    Year > 2025,                          % not needed here, but keeps rule clean
    agi(Taxpayer, Year, AGI),             % AGI must be known
    filing_status(Taxpayer, Year, Status),% filing status must be known
    phaseout_threshold(Status, Threshold),
    base_exemption_amount(Base),
    (  AGI =< Threshold                   % no phase-out applies
    -> Amount = Base
    ;  percentage_over(AGI, Threshold, Percent),
       Reduced is Base * (1 - Percent/100),
       Amount is max(0, round(Reduced))   % simplistic rounding
    ),
    \+ deduction_allowed_to_other(Taxpayer, Year).

/* phase-out thresholds from §68(b)                                    */
phaseout_threshold(joint,        300000).
phaseout_threshold(surviving,    300000).
phaseout_threshold(head,         275000).
phaseout_threshold(single,       250000).
phaseout_threshold(separate,     150000).   % 1/2 of joint, by statute

/* helper: compute percent reduction – minimal implementation          */
percentage_over(AGI, Threshold, 100) :- AGI  >= Threshold +  50000, !. % cap at 100 %
percentage_over(AGI, Threshold, P)  :-
    Excess is AGI - Threshold,
    Steps is ceil(Excess / 2500),
    P is min(100, Steps * 2).

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */
taxpayer(alice).
year(2015).

/* Alice is entitled to a personal exemption for herself in 2015      */
entitled_personal_exemption(alice, 2015).

/* No other taxpayer may claim Alice as a dependent                    */
deduction_allowed_to_other(_SomeOther, _Year) :- fail.

/* NOTE:  The case gives no information about Alice’s AGI or filing
          status, so there are deliberately NO facts agi/3 or
          filing_status/3.                                             */

/* ---------- Claim verification ---------- */
:- (  exemption_amount(alice, 2015, 2000)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

reduced_exemption_amount(Person, ReducedAmount) :-

text: Alice's income in 2015 is $395276. The applicable amount according to section 68(b) is $250000.
question: Under section 151(d)(3)(B), the applicable percentage for Alice for 2015 is equal to 118.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules needed for this question ---------- */

/*  Section 151(d)(3)(B) – “applicable percentage” computation

    1. Determine by how much the taxpayer’s adjusted gross income
       (“income/Inc”) exceeds the applicable amount (“App”).
    2. For every $2,500 (or fraction thereof) of excess, add 2 percentage
       points.
    3. Cap the result at 100 percentage points.
*/

applicable_percentage(Person, Percentage) :-
    income(Person, Inc),
    applicable_amount(Person, App),
    Excess is Inc - App,
    Excess > 0,
    Portions is ceiling(Excess / 2500),
    Temp is Portions * 2,
    ( Temp > 100 -> Percentage = 100 ; Percentage = Temp ).

/*  If the excess is zero or negative, the percentage is 0.             */
applicable_percentage(Person, 0) :-
    income(Person, Inc),
    applicable_amount(Person, App),
    Inc =< App.

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

income(alice, 395276).
applicable_amount(alice, 250000).

/* ---------- The claim to be verified ---------- */

claim_applicable_percentage(alice, 118).

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */

verify_claim :-
    claim_applicable_percentage(Person, Claimed),
    applicable_percentage(Person, Claimed).

:- (  verify_claim
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

reference_prolog:

/* ---------- Statutory rules we need ---------- */

/* filing status derived from marital state in this problem */

status(alice, single).

/* Applicable amount under §68(b) */
applicable_amount(single, 250000).
applicable_amount(joint_or_surviving_spouse, 300000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household, 275000).
applicable_amount(married_filing_separately, 150000).

/* Adjusted gross income given in the case */
agi(alice, 276932).

/* §151(d)(3)(B): applicable percentage  = 2 percentage points
   for each $2,500 (or fraction thereof) by which AGI exceeds
   the applicable amount, capped at 100.                                   */

applicable_percentage(Taxpayer, Percent) :-
    agi(Taxpayer, AGI),
    status(Taxpayer, Status),
    applicable_amount(Status, Limit),
    Excess is AGI - Limit,
    Excess =< 0,                             % No excess → 0%
    Percent = 0.

applicable_percentage(Taxpayer, Percent) :-
    agi(Taxpayer, AGI),
    status(Taxpayer, Status),
    applicable_amount(Status, Limit),
    Excess is AGI - Limit,
    Excess > 0,
    CeilUnits is ceiling(Excess / 2500),
    RawPercent is CeilUnits * 2,
    ( RawPercent > 100 -> Percent = 100
    ; Percent = RawPercent ).

/* ---------- Facts from the claim to be verified ---------- */

claimed_applicable_percentage(alice, 22).

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */
verify_claim :-
    claimed_applicable_percentage(alice, Claimed),
    applicable_percentage(alice, Claimed).

:- (  verify_claim
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* =========  Statutory rules (simplified to what is relevant here)  ========= */

/* Section 152(a): A person is a dependent of a taxpayer if the person is
   (1) the taxpayer’s qualifying child, or
   (2) the taxpayer’s qualifying relative.                                */

dependent(Person, Taxpayer) :-
    qualifying_child(Person, Taxpayer),
    \+ disqualified_qc(Person, Taxpayer).   % placeholder for any §152(b) bar.

dependent(Person, Taxpayer) :-
    qualifying_relative(Person, Taxpayer).

/* ----------  Qualifying-relative test (only the statutory element needed) --
   §152(d)(1)(D): an individual is NOT a qualifying relative if the individual
   is the qualifying child of ANY taxpayer.                                 */

qualifying_relative(Person, Taxpayer) :-
    relationship(Person, Taxpayer, relative),
    no_income(Person),                     % §152(d)(1)(B) (irrelevant here)
    \+ qualifying_child(Person, _).        % barred if QC of anyone.

/* No concrete disqualifying facts are given, so leave this always false. */
disqualified_qc(_,_) :- fail.


/* ==========================  Case facts  =========================== */

qualifying_child(bob, charlie).   % Given: Bob is Charlie’s qualifying child.

    that person is a “dependent” of that taxpayer for that year.     */

dependent(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year),
    \+ married_joint_return(Child, Year).   /* no joint-return exception */

    whenever the individual is a dependent of some taxpayer

section_152b1_applies(Individual, Year) :-

/***********************
 *  Statutory rules   *
 ***********************/

% Section 152(b)(2) applies to an individual for a year
% if that individual filed a joint return with the individual's spouse
% for that taxable year.

section152_b2_applies(Person, Year) :-
    married(Person, Spouse, Year),
    filed_joint_return(Person, Spouse, Year).

/***********************
 *  Auxiliary rules    *
 ***********************/

% filed_joint_return/3 holds when Person1 and Person2 together filed a
% joint return for Year (order-insensitive).

filed_joint_return(P1, P2, Year) :-
    joint_return(Filers, Year),
    member(P1, Filers),
    member(P2, Filers),
    P1 \= P2.

% The married/3 relation is taken to be symmetric.

married(P1, P2, Year) :-
    marriage_date(P1, P2, Date),
    date_year(Date, Y0),
    Y0 =< Year,
    P1 \= P2.

married(P1, P2, Year) :-
    married(P2, P1, Year).

/***********************
 *  Case facts         *
 ***********************/

% Alice and Bob got married on 1 Jan 2015
marriage_date(alice, bob, date(2015,1,1)).

% They filed a joint return for 2015
joint_return([alice, bob], 2015).

% Helper to extract the year component of a date
date_year(date(Y, _, _), Y).

/***********************
 *  Verification goal  *
 ***********************/

:- (  section152_b2_applies(alice, 2015)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

% ---------- Statutory rule (simplified for 152(c)(1)(B)) ----------
% Section 152(c)(1)(B) is satisfied for Child with respect to Taxpayer
% in Year if the child has the same principal place of abode as the
% taxpayer for MORE THAN one-half of that taxable year.

sec152c1B_applies(Taxpayer,Child,Year) :-
        more_than_half_year_same_abode(Taxpayer,Child,Year).

% “More than one-half of the year”  -> more than 6 of the 12 months
more_than_half_year_same_abode(Taxpayer,Child,Year) :-
        findall(Month,
                same_home_month(Taxpayer,Child,Year,Month),
                Months),
        length(Months,Count),
        Count > 6.   % strictly more than 6 months

% ---------- Facts from the case ----------
% Alice and Bob lived in the same home from 1 Sept 2015 to 3 Nov 2019.
% So, for 2015 they shared September, October, November, December.

same_home_month(alice,bob,2015,9).
same_home_month(alice,bob,2015,10).
same_home_month(alice,bob,2015,11).
same_home_month(alice,bob,2015,12).

% (Months for later years are irrelevant to the 2015 determination.)

% ---------- Verification goal ----------
:- (  sec152c1B_applies(alice,bob,2015)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).

/**********************************************************************
 Statutory rules (only those needed for this determination)
**********************************************************************/

% Section 152(c)(1)(B):
% The qualifying-child test in subparagraph (B) is satisfied for
% taxpayer T, child C, and year Y if C has the same principal place
% of abode as T for more than one-half of taxable year Y.

sec152c1b(T, C, Y) :-
    abode_more_than_half_year(T, C, Y).

/**********************************************************************
 Factual predicates extracted from the case
**********************************************************************/

% Alice and Bob lived in the same home from 1 Sep 2015 through 3 Nov 2019.
lived_same_home(alice, bob,
                date(2015,9,1),
                date(2019,11,3)).

% Helper:  true if the “lived together” interval entirely covers year Y,
% which certainly implies co-residence for more than half of that year.
covers_full_year(date(FromY, FromM, FromD),
                 date(ToY,   ToM,   ToD),
                 Y) :-
    % start date is on/before 1 Jan Y
    (FromY  <  Y ;
     FromY == Y, FromM =< 1, FromD =< 1),
    % end date is on/after 31 Dec Y
    (ToY  >  Y ;
     ToY == Y, ToM >= 12, ToD >= 31).

% An abode is shared for more than half of year Y if an asserted
% lived_same_home interval covers the full year.
abode_more_than_half_year(T, C, Y) :-
    lived_same_home(T, C, From, To),
    covers_full_year(From, To, Y).

/**********************************************************************
 Verification goal
**********************************************************************/

:- (  sec152c1b(alice, bob, 2016)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

/* ---------- Statutory rules (relevant excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 2(b)(1): Head of household definition */
head_of_household(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    not(married_at_end_of_year(Taxpayer, Year)),
    not(surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year)),
    (
        (
            maintains_household_for_period(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year, Period),
            qualifying_child(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year),
            Period > 0.5
        )
        ;
        (
            maintains_household_for_period(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year, _),
            dependent_of_taxpayer(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year),
            entitled_to_deduction(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year),
            father_or_mother(Dependent, Taxpayer)
        )
    ),
    furnished_over_half_cost(Taxpayer, Year).

/* Section 2(b)(1)(B): Maintains household as principal place of abode for father or mother, deduction allowed under section 151 */
section_2b1B_applies(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    maintains_household_for_period(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year, _),
    principal_place_of_abode(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year),
    father_or_mother(Dependent, Taxpayer),
    entitled_to_deduction(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year),
    furnished_over_half_cost(Taxpayer, Year).

/* Relationship: father_or_mother/2 is true if Dependent is the father or mother of Taxpayer. */

/* ---------- Case facts ---------- */

/* Family structure */
married(alice, bob, 1992).
child_of(charlie, alice).
child_of(charlie, bob).
born(charlie, date(2000,10,9)).
died(alice, date(2014,7,9)).

/* Bob and Charlie lived together; Bob furnished household costs from 2004 to 2019 */
furnished_over_half_cost(bob, Year) :- Year >= 2004, Year =< 2019.
lived_together(bob, charlie, Year) :- Year >= 2004, Year =< 2019.
principal_place_of_abode(charlie, bob, Year) :- lived_together(bob, charlie, Year).
maintains_household_for_period(bob, charlie, Year, 1.0) :- lived_together(bob, charlie, Year).

/* Bob entitled to deduction for Charlie under section 151(c) for 2015-2019 */
entitled_to_deduction(bob, charlie, Year) :- Year >= 2015, Year =< 2019.

/* Charlie is Bob's son, so not his father/mother */
father_or_mother(X, Y) :- fail.  /* No such relationship for Bob and Charlie */

/* Section 2(b)(1)(B) reference: checks for head of household via maintaining home for father/mother with deduction */
dependent_of_taxpayer(charlie, bob, Year) :- entitled_to_deduction(bob, charlie, Year).

/* Marital/surviving spouse status for Bob in 2018 */

/* Alice died in 2014, so for years 2015+ Bob is not a surviving spouse (surviving spouse only for 2 years after death) */
surviving_spouse(bob, Year) :- alice_died_year(DeathYear), (Year =:= DeathYear; Year =:= DeathYear + 1), maintains_household_for_dependent(bob, Year).
alice_died_year(2014).

maintains_household_for_dependent(bob, Year) :-
    maintains_household_for_period(bob, Dep, Year, Period),
    Period > 0,
    entitled_to_deduction(bob, Dep, Year),
    son_or_daughter(Dep, bob).

son_or_daughter(Dep, Taxpayer) :- child_of(Dep, Taxpayer).

/* Marital status: Alice died in 2014, so Bob is unmarried after that; no evidence of remarriage */
married_at_end_of_year(bob, Year) :- Year =< 2014.

/* ---------- Query to test if section 2(b)(1)(B) applies to Bob in 2018 ---------- */

% Section 2(b)(1)(B) requires Bob to maintain as principal abode the household for his father or mother (for whom he is entitled to a deduction).
% In the facts, Bob maintains for his son Charlie, not for his father or mother.

:- (  section_2b1B_applies(bob, 2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

% Section 152(c)(2)(B): an individual bears the described relationship
% to the taxpayer if the individual is a brother, sister, step-brother,

relationship_152c2b(Individual, Taxpayer) :- sister(Individual, Taxpayer).

*/

% Relationship test for qualifying child

% (A) A child of the taxpayer or descendant of such a child
bears_relationship_152c2(Person, Taxpayer) :-
    child(Person, Taxpayer).
bears_relationship_152c2(Person, Taxpayer) :-
    descendant(Person, Taxpayer).

% (B) A brother, sister, stepbrother, or stepsister of the taxpayer
bears_relationship_152c2(Person, Taxpayer) :-
    sibling(Person, Taxpayer).

% A descendant of such a sibling
bears_relationship_152c2(Person, Taxpayer) :-
    descendant_of_sibling(Person, Taxpayer).

% ---------- Auxiliary family‐relation rules ----------
descendant(Person, Ancestor) :-
    child(Person, Ancestor).
descendant(Person, Ancestor) :-
    child(Person, Parent),
    descendant(Parent, Ancestor).

sibling(Person1, Person2) :-
    parent(Parent, Person1),
    parent(Parent, Person2),
    Person1 \= Person2.

descendant_of_sibling(Person, Taxpayer) :-
    sibling(Ancestor, Taxpayer),
    descendant(Person, Ancestor).

% ---------- Facts from the case ----------
child(bob, alice).

% Derive parent relationship (inverse of child)
parent(Parent, Child) :-
    child(Child, Parent).

% ---------- Verification ----------
:- (  bears_relationship_152c2(bob, alice)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

birth(bob,   1984, 1, 31).

/* Helper: younger(Person1, Person2) is true when Person1 is younger
   than Person2 (i.e., born in a later calendar year).                 */
younger(P1, P2) :-
    birth(P1, Y1, _, _),
    birth(P2, Y2, _, _),
    Y1 > Y2.

/* Helper: age(Person, TaxYear, AgeAtEndOfYear).  We assume age as of the
   end of the taxable year (December 31), so month/day do not affect the
   simple difference in years.                                          */
age(Person, TaxYear, Age) :-
    birth(Person, BirthYear, _, _),
    Age is TaxYear - BirthYear.

/* Section 152(c)(3) age‐requirement satisfaction predicate           */
s152c3_age_requirement(Taxpayer, Child, TaxYear) :-
    younger(Child, Taxpayer),
    age(Child, TaxYear, AgeChild),
    AgeChild < 25.

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

verify_claim :-
    tax_year(Y),
    s152c3_age_requirement(alice, bob, Y).

/* ---------- Produce the required answer ---------- */
:- (  verify_claim
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')

   - AND maintains a household that is principal abode of a child/stepchild/dependent (§2(a)(1)(B)),

stepmother(Stepmother, Child) :-

    male(Stepfather),

    ( father(Parent, Child)

%                                LowerLimit, UpperLimit, BaseTax, MarginalRate).
% Limits are inclusive of the lower limit and exclusive of the upper limit,
% except that UpperLimit = inf means there is no upper cap.

/* tax_bracket_hoh/3
   tax_bracket_hoh(TaxableIncome , TaxOwed , Section1bBracketIndex )
   Implements the 2017 §1(b) head-of-household rate schedule            */
tax_bracket_hoh(TI, Tax,  i) :- TI =< 29600 ,               Tax is        0.15 * TI.
tax_bracket_hoh(TI, Tax, ii) :- TI =< 76400 , TI > 29600  , Tax is  4440 + 0.28 * (TI - 29600).
tax_bracket_hoh(TI, Tax, iii):- TI =< 127500, TI > 76400  , Tax is 17544 + 0.31 * (TI - 76400).
tax_bracket_hoh(TI, Tax, iv) :- TI =< 250000, TI > 127500 , Tax is 33385 + 0.36 * (TI - 127500).
tax_bracket_hoh(TI, Tax, v)  :- TI  > 250000              , Tax is 77485 + 0.396* (TI - 250000).

/* overall tax_owed/3 – only the head of household case required here   */

   Alice is married (sec. 7703) and files a separate return.        */
filing_status(alice, married_filing_separate).

/* Alice’s taxable income for 2017 */
taxable_income(alice, 28864).

/* Tax-rate schedule for §1(d) (married individuals filing separate returns)       */

tax_bracket(mfs, 1, 0,      18450, base(0),           rate(0.15)).
tax_bracket(mfs, 2, 18450,  44575, base(2767.50),     rate(0.28)).
tax_bracket(mfs, 3, 44575,  70000, base(10082.50),    rate(0.31)).
tax_bracket(mfs, 4, 70000, 125000, base(17964.25),    rate(0.36)).
tax_bracket(mfs, 5, 125000, inf,   base(37764.25),    rate(0.396)).

/* ---------- Derived computations ---------- */

/* locate the proper bracket for a given taxable income */
applicable_bracket(Status, TI, BracketNum, Lower, Upper, Base, Rate) :-
    tax_bracket(Status, BracketNum, Lower, Upper, base(Base), rate(Rate)),
    (Upper == inf -> TI > Lower
    ;               TI =< Upper, TI > Lower).

tax_due(Person, Tax, BracketNum) :-
    filing_status(Person, married_filing_separate),
    taxable_income(Person, TI),
    applicable_bracket(mfs, TI, BracketNum, Lower, _Upper, Base, Rate),
    Excess is TI - Lower,
    Tax is Base + Rate * Excess.

    round(TaxExact) =:= 5683,
    BracketNum =:= 4.   /* §1(d)(iv) corresponds to bracket number 4 */

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */

    person(Person),
    return_type(Person, separate),
    income(Person, Income),
    tax_married_separate(Income, Tax).

    Diff is abs(A-B),
    Diff < 1.          /* treat ordinary rounding to the nearest dollar as equal */

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

      approx_equal(Tax, 33653)

   ).

% applies_2a1a(Taxpayer, TaxYear) is true if the taxpayer's spouse
% died during either of the two years immediately preceding the taxable year.

applies_2a1a(Taxpayer, TaxYear) :-
    spouse_of(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    spouse_died(Spouse, DeathYear),
    DeathYear < TaxYear,                % death must be before the taxable year
    Diff is TaxYear - DeathYear,
    Diff =< 2.                          % within the two immediately preceding years

/* ---------- Symmetric spouse relation derived from marriage fact ---------- */
spouse_of(Person1, Person2) :-
    married(Person1, Person2, _).
spouse_of(Person1, Person2) :-
    married(Person2, Person1, _).

/* ---------- Case facts ---------- */

spouse_died(alice, 2014).

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */
:- (  applies_2a1a(bob, 2014)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).

```

/* ---------- Statutory rules (simplified to what is relevant) ---------- */

/* Section 2(a)(1)(B) applies to a taxpayer in a taxable year if:
      1. The taxpayer maintains a household which is the principal place of
         abode for the taxable year of a dependent listed in section 152
         who is a son, stepson, daughter, or stepdaughter;
      2. The taxpayer is entitled to a deduction for that dependent under
         section 151 for that taxable year.                                              */

sec2a1b_applies(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    maintains_household(Taxpayer,Year),
    principal_place_of_abode(Taxpayer,Dependent,Year),
    dependent_of(Taxpayer,Dependent,Year),
    child_of_taxpayer(Dependent,Taxpayer).

/* ---------- Section-151 deduction for a dependent ---------- */

deduction_under_151(Taxpayer,Dependent,Year) :-
    dependent_of(Taxpayer,Dependent,Year).

/* ---------- Section-152 dependent rules (only those needed here) ---------- */

/* An individual cannot be a dependent if the individual filed a joint
   return with the individual's spouse for that year ( §152(b)(2) ).        */

dependent_of(_Taxpayer,Individual,Year) :-
    joint_return_filed(Individual,Year),            % married filing joint
    !, fail.

dependent_of(Taxpayer,Individual,_Year) :-
    qualifying_child_of(Taxpayer,Individual).

/* ---------- Qualifying child (only relationship & abode tests needed) --- */

qualifying_child_of(Taxpayer,Child) :-
    child_of_taxpayer(Child,Taxpayer).

principal_place_of_abode(bob,charlie,2020).

joint_return_filed(charlie,2020).

birth_year(charlie,2000).

filed_joint_return(_Person,_Year) :- fail.   /* closed-world assumption */

qualifying_child(Taxpayer,Child,Year) :-
        child_of(Child,Taxpayer),                         % relationship

        age(Taxpayer,Year,AgeT), AgeC < AgeT,             % younger than taxpayer

Section 152(b)(2) applies to Charlie as the dependent and Bob as the taxpayer for 2018.
question: Section 2(b)(1)(A)(i)(II) applies to Bob with Charlie as the qualifying child in 2018.

```prolog
/* ----------  Statutory rules (simplified to the predicates we need) ---------- */

/* 152(c): qualifying child */
qualifying_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    relationship_152c(Child, Taxpayer),
    principal_abode_same(Child, Taxpayer, Year),
    age_under_25(Child, Year),
    \+ joint_return_filed(Child, Year).   /* 152(c)(1)(E) */

/* relationship test – 152(c)(2)(A) (child or descendant of a child) */
relationship_152c(Child, Taxpayer) :-
    child_of(Child, Taxpayer).

/* same principal place of abode for > ½ the year – given for every whole year
   they live together in the facts                              */
principal_abode_same(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    household(Taxpayer, Child, Year).

/* age test – “younger than 25” (152(c)(3))  */
age_under_25(Person, Year) :-
    birth_year(Person, Birth),
    Age is Year - Birth,
    Age < 25.

/* joint return filed with spouse (negates qualifying-child status
   via 152(c)(1)(E) and triggers §152(b)(2))                      */
joint_return_filed(Person, Year) :-
    married(Person, _, MarryYear),
    MarryYear =< Year.   /* assume a joint return is filed for the
                            first and every subsequent married year */

/* §152(b)(2) applies when joint return filed                       */
section_152b2_applies(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year) :-
    joint_return_filed(Dependent, Year),
    Taxpayer \= Dependent.

/* Dependent test (allowed only if qualifying_child and no §152(b)(2) bar) */
dependent_of(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year),
    \+ section_152b2_applies(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year).

/* §2(b)(1)(A)(i)(II) applies  */
section_2b1A_i_II_applies(Taxpayer, Child, Year) :-
    qualifying_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year),          % “a qualifying child …”
    married_at_close(Child, Year),                    % clause (I)
    section_152b2_applies(Taxpayer, Child, Year),     % clause (II)
    \+ dependent_of(Taxpayer, Child, Year).           % “is not a dependent …”

/* helper: married at close of year                    */
married_at_close(Person, Year) :-
    married(Person, _, MarryYear),
    MarryYear =< Year.

/* ----------  Facts from the case  ---------- */

/* family relations */
child_of(charlie, bob).

/* dates of birth (only Charlie needed for age test) */
birth_year(charlie, 2000).

/* marriages */
married(bob, alice, 1992).
spouse_death(alice, 2014).

married(charlie, dan, 2018).

/* household – Bob and Charlie live together 2004-2019, Bob pays >½ cost */
household(bob, charlie, Year) :-
    Year >= 2004,
    Year =< 2019.

furnishes_cost(bob, Year) :-
    Year >= 2004,
    Year =< 2019.

/* ----------  Verification query  ---------- */

:- (  section_2b1A_i_II_applies(bob, charlie, 2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. Charlie married Dan on Feb 14th, 2018. Section 152(b)(2) applies to Bob as the dependent and Charlie as the taxpayer for 2018.
question: Section 2(b)(1)(A)(i) applies to Bob in 2018.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ----------  Statutory rules (simplified to what is needed here) ---------- */

/*  Section 7703 – marital status at the close of the taxable year          */
married_at_end(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    current_spouse(Taxpayer,Year,_).

/*  “Current spouse” – there is one only if the marriage is still in force   */
current_spouse(A,Year,B) :-
    spouse(A,B,StartYear,EndYear),
    (  var(EndYear) -> Year >= StartYear
    ;  Year >= StartYear, Year =< EndYear ).

/*  §2(a) – surviving-spouse status applies only for two years after death   */
surviving_spouse(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    spouse(Taxpayer,Spouse,_,DeathYear),
    nonvar(DeathYear),
    Year =< DeathYear + 2,
    dependent_child_in_year(Taxpayer,Year).

/*  A dependent child exists if there is at least one child who is a
    dependent of the taxpayer for the year (very rough abstraction).        */
dependent_child_in_year(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    child_of(Child,Taxpayer),
    dependent_of(Child,Taxpayer,Year).

/*  §152(c) – qualifying child (only the elements we need)                  */
qualifying_child(Child,Taxpayer,Year) :-
    child_of(Child,Taxpayer),
    same_abode_half_year(Child,Taxpayer,Year),
    under_twenty_five(Child,Year),
    \+ filed_joint_return(Child,_,Year).   % 152(c)(1)(E)

/*  §2(b)(1)(A)(i) – the head-of-household child test                        */
applies_2b1Ai(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    \+ married_at_end(Taxpayer,Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Taxpayer,Year),
    furnishes_half_cost(Taxpayer,Year),
    qualifying_child(Child,Taxpayer,Year),
    (   \+ ( child_married_at_end(Child,Year),
             \+ dependent_of(Child,Taxpayer,Year),
             denied_only_by_152b2(Child,Taxpayer,Year)
           )
    ).

/*  Helper predicates ----------------------------------------------------- */
child_married_at_end(Child,Year) :-
    married_in_year(Child,Year).

/*  “Denied only by 152(b)(2)”  – here we model it as: he is not a dependent
    and the reason is a joint return with the spouse.                       */
denied_only_by_152b2(Child,_,Year) :-
    filed_joint_return(Child,_,Year).

/*  A person is a dependent of another in a year unless 152(b)(2) forbids.  */
dependent_of(Person,Taxpayer,Year) :-
    \+ filed_joint_return(Person,_,Year),
    % other dependency tests ignored for this problem
    Person \= Taxpayer.

/*  Furnishing >½ of the household cost (given as a fact below)             */
furnishes_half_cost(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    furnished_costs(Taxpayer,From,To),
    Year >= From, Year =< To.

/*  Same principal place of abode for >½ year (given as a fact below)       */
same_abode_half_year(PersonA,PersonB,Year) :-
    lived_together(PersonA,PersonB,From,To),
    Year >= From, Year =< To.

/*  Age test: the individual is <25 at the end of the year                  */
under_twenty_five(Person,Year) :-
    birth_year(Person,BY),
    Age is Year - BY,
    Age < 25.

/* ----------  Case facts -------------------------------------------------- */

person(alice).  person(bob).  person(charlie).  person(dan).

spouse(bob,alice,1992,2014).        % Alice died in 2014
death(alice,2014).

child_of(charlie,bob).
child_of(charlie,alice).

birth_year(charlie,2000).

lived_together(charlie,bob,2004,2019).
furnished_costs(bob,2004,2019).

married_in_year(charlie,2018).
spouse(charlie,dan,2018).           % marriage begins 2018, no end year yet

filed_joint_return(charlie,dan,2018).   % Charlie & Dan filed jointly in 2018

/* ----------  Query ------------------------------------------------------- */

:- (  applies_2b1Ai(bob,2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

In 2017, Charlie filed a joint return with his spouse whom he married on Dec 1st, 2016.

```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules (simplified for this problem) ---------- */

/* age calculation */
age(Person, Year, Age) :-
    born(Person, BirthYear),
    Age is Year - BirthYear.

/* Relationship rules needed for this case */
relationship_qc(Person, Taxpayer) :-                % §152(c)(2)(B) brother etc.
    brother(Person, Taxpayer).
relationship_qr(Person, Taxpayer) :-                % §152(d)(2)(B) brother etc.
    brother(Person, Taxpayer).

/* Joint-return test for qualifying child – any joint return other than
   merely to claim a refund disqualifies (§152(c)(1)(E))                */
joint_return_nonrefund(Person, Year) :-
    joint_return(Person, _Spouse, Year).

/* Qualifying child test (§152(c))  */
qualifies_child(Child, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    relationship_qc(Child, Taxpayer),
    same_home(Taxpayer, Child, Year),
    age(Child, Year, ChildAge),
    age(Taxpayer, Year, TP_Age),
    ChildAge < 25,
    ChildAge < TP_Age,
    \+ joint_return_nonrefund(Child, Year).

/* Qualifying relative test (§152(d))                                   */
qualifies_relative(Rel, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    relationship_qr(Rel, Taxpayer),
    income(Rel, Year, Inc),
    Inc =:= 0,
    \+ (qualifies_child(Rel, _, Year)).   % not a qualifying child of anyone

/* Dependent if either test satisfied (§152(a))                         */
dependent(Person, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifies_child(Person, Taxpayer, Year).
dependent(Person, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifies_relative(Person, Taxpayer, Year).

/* Head-of-household provision §2(b)(1)(A)(ii) (only elements relevant
   to this dispute are modelled)                                         */
section_2_b_1_A_ii_applies(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    same_home(Taxpayer, Dependent, Year),                   % principal place
    household_cost_majority(Taxpayer, Year),                % over half costs
    dependent(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year),                   % §151 deduction
    YearPart is 1, YearPart = 1.   /* dummy succeed – placeholders */

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

/* Birth years (approximate; only relative ordering matters here) */
born(bob, 1970).
born(charlie, 2000).

/* Family relationship */
brother(charlie, bob).

/* Charlie’s marriage and joint return */
married(charlie, dana, date(2016,12,1)).      % spouse name arbitrary ‘dana’
joint_return(charlie, dana, 2017).

/* Home-sharing and support 2004-2019 */
same_home(bob, charlie, Year) :-
    Year >= 2004, Year =< 2019.
same_home(charlie, bob, Year) :-
    same_home(bob, charlie, Year).

household_cost_majority(bob, Year) :-
    Year >= 2004, Year =< 2019.

/* Charlie’s income in 2017 */
income(charlie, 2017, 312489).

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */

:- (  section_2_b_1_A_ii_applies(charlie, bob, 2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

head_of_household_2b1A(Taxpayer,Year) :-

maintains_dependent_household(Taxpayer,Year) :-

From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. Charlie was a qualifying child of Bob under section 152(c) from 2004 to 2019.
question: Section 2(b)(1)(A) applies to Bob in 2018.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
% ---------- Facts from the case ----------
person(bob).
person(alice).
person(charlie).

spouse(bob,alice).
spouse(alice,bob).
marriage_date(bob,alice,date(1992,2,3)).

death(alice,date(2014,7,9)).

% Charlie's birth and relationship to Bob
birth(charlie,date(2000,10,9)).
child_of(charlie,bob).

% Bob furnished over half the household costs from 2004-2019
furnished_costs(bob,Year) :- Year>=2004, Year=<2019.

% Charlie lived with Bob and was Bob's qualifying child 2004-2019
qualifying_child(charlie,bob,Year) :- Year>=2004, Year=<2019.

% ---------- Generic rules drawn from the statutes ----------
% spouse_alive(Person,Year) is true if the spouse of Person is alive at the close of Year.
spouse_alive(Spouse,Year) :-
    death(Spouse,date(DY,_,_)),
    DY > Year.

% Someone is married at the close of the year if he has a living spouse.  (No divorce facts are given.)
married(Person,Year) :-
    spouse(Person,Spouse),
    spouse_alive(Spouse,Year).

% Not married means the negation of married (section 2(b)(1))
not_married(Person,Year) :- \+ married(Person,Year).

% Surviving spouse status: spouse died in one of the two years immediately preceding the taxable year
surviving_spouse(Person,Year) :-
    spouse(Person,Spouse),
    death(Spouse,date(DY,_,_)),
    Gap is Year-DY,
    Gap>=1, Gap=<2,
    % must also maintain a household for a qualifying child (given in the statute)
    furnished_costs(Person,Year),
    qualifying_child(_,Person,Year).

not_surviving_spouse(Person,Year) :- \+ surviving_spouse(Person,Year).

% Maintains household for > half the year with a qualifying child (section 2(b)(1)(A)(i))
maintains_household_with_qual_child(Person,Year) :-
    furnished_costs(Person,Year),
    qualifying_child(Child,Person,Year),
    child_of(Child,Person).

% Head-of-household under section 2(b)(1)(A)
head_of_household_A(Person,Year) :-
    not_married(Person,Year),
    not_surviving_spouse(Person,Year),
    maintains_household_with_qual_child(Person,Year).

% ---------- Verification goal ----------
:- (  head_of_household_A(bob,2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob got married on Feb 3rd, 1992. Alice and Bob have a child, Charlie, born October 9th, 2000. Alice died on July 9th, 2014. From 2004 to 2019, Bob furnished the costs of maintaining the home where he and Charlie lived during that time. Bob is entitled to a deduction for Charlie under section 151(c) for the years 2015 to 2019.
question: Section 2(b)(1)(B) applies to Bob in 2018.
reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

% Marriage and child
married(alice, bob).
spouse(alice, bob).
spouse(bob, alice).

child(charlie, bob).

% Alice died on July 9, 2014 (during 2014)
death_year(alice, 2014).

% Bob maintained the household (costs > 50%) for the relevant period
maintains_household(bob, Year) :- integer(Year), Year >= 2004, Year =< 2019.

% Bob is entitled to a deduction for Charlie under section 151(c) for 2015-2019
deduction_for(bob, charlie, Year) :- integer(Year), Year >= 2015, Year =< 2019.

% Bob is not a nonresident alien (relevant for section 2(b)(3)(A))
nonresident_alien(bob, Year) :- fail.

/* ---------- Statutory definitions (encoded as predicates) ---------- */

% §7703(a): At close of the taxable year, Bob is married if his spouse is alive then.
married_at_close_of_year(Person, Year) :-
    spouse(Person, Spouse),
    \+ death_year(Spouse, Year).

% §7703(c): Special rule for certain married individuals living apart (not needed here)
% We encode it as always failing for this simplified case.
married_individual_living_apart(Person, Year) :- fail.

% §2(b)(2)(C): If spouse died during the taxable year, individual is considered married at close.
considered_married_due_to_spouse_death(Person, Year) :-
    spouse(Person, Spouse),
    death_year(Spouse, Year).

% §2(b)(2)(A)-(B): Legal separation / nonresident spouse rules (not used here)
considered_not_married_due_to_legal_separation(Person, Year) :- fail.
considered_not_married_due_to_nonresident_spouse(Person, Year) :- fail.

% §2(b)(1): Head of household requires not married and not a surviving spouse.
% We also encode the "maintains household" condition in (1)(A) and (1)(B).
head_of_household(Person, Year) :-
    \+ ( considered_married_due_to_spouse_death(Person, Year)
       ; married_individual_living_apart(Person, Year)
       ; married_at_close_of_year(Person, Year)
       ; considered_not_married_due_to_legal_separation(Person, Year)
       ; considered_not_married_due_to_nonresident_spouse(Person, Year)
       ),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year),
    maintains_household(Person, Year),
    (
        % §2(b)(1)(A): Principal place of abode of a qualifying child (or other dependent)
        ( qualifies_as_qualifying_child(charlie, bob, Year),
          principal_place_abode(charlie, bob, Year),
          entitled_to_deduction(bob, charlie, Year)
        )
    ;
        % §2(b)(1)(B): Principal place of abode of father/mother (not used here)
        fail
    ).

% §2(b)(3)(A): Head of household cannot be a nonresident alien
head_of_household(Person, Year) :-
    nonresident_alien(Person, Year),
    !,
    fail.

% §2(a)(1): Surviving spouse definition
surviving_spouse(Person, Year) :-
    spouse(Person, DeceasedSpouse),
    death_year(DeceasedSpouse, DeathYear),
    ( DeathYear =:= Year - 1 ; DeathYear =:= Year - 2 ),
    maintains_household(Person, Year),
    entitled_to_deduction(Person, ChildDependent, Year),
    dependent_relationship(ChildDependent, Person, son_or_daughter),
    \+ remarried_before_close_of_year(Person, Year),
    joint_return_possible_for_death_year(Person, DeathYear).

% §2(a)(2)(A): No surviving spouse if remarried before close of year
remarried_before_close_of_year(Person, Year) :- fail.

% §2(a)(2)(B): Joint return possible for the year spouse died (simplified)
joint_return_possible_for_death_year(Person, DeathYear) :-
    spouse(Person, _),
    DeathYear =:= DeathYear.

% §152(c): Qualifying child (subset sufficient for this case)
qualifies_as_qualifying_child(Child, Parent, Year) :-
    child(Child, Parent),
    same_principal_place_abode(Child, Parent, Year),
    age_less_than_25(Child, Year),
    \+ filed_joint_return_with_spouse(Child, Year).

same_principal_place_abode(Child, Parent, Year) :-
    principal_place_abode(Child, Parent, Year).

age_less_than_25(Child, Year) :-
    birth_year(Child, BirthYear),
    Age is Year - BirthYear,
    Age < 25.

filed_joint_return_with_spouse(Child, Year) :- fail.

% §152(c)(2)(A): Child relationship
dependent_relationship(Child, Parent, son_or_daughter) :-
    child(Child, Parent).

% §151(c): Entitled to a deduction for a dependent (subset sufficient for this case)
entitled_to_deduction(Person, Dependent, Year) :-
    deduction_for(Person, Dependent, Year).

% Household/principal place of abode facts (derived from case facts)
principal_place_abode(charlie, bob, Year) :-
    maintains_household(bob, Year).

% Birth year (from case facts)
birth_year(charlie, 2000).

/* ---------- Claim-specific check ---------- */

claim_statement :-
    head_of_household(bob, 2018).

/* ---------- Query and output ---------- */

:- (  claim_statement
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

```prolog
/*****************************************************************
  Statutory rules (only the portions needed for this problem)
*****************************************************************/

% A taxpayer is “unmarried” for a taxable year if (a) the taxpayer
% had a spouse who has died before that year and (b) the taxpayer
% has not remarried before the close of the year.
unmarried(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    spouse(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    spouse_death(Taxpayer, Spouse, DeathYear),
    DeathYear  <  Year,
    \+ remarried(Taxpayer, Year).

% A taxpayer is a “surviving spouse” under §2(a) for a taxable year
% if the spouse died in either of the two years immediately
% preceding the taxable year.
surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    spouse(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    spouse_death(Taxpayer, Spouse, DeathYear),
    Diff is Year - DeathYear,
    Diff >= 1,
    Diff =< 2.

% A taxpayer “maintains a household” for a parent in a given year
% if the taxpayer furnishes over half the cost of that household
% for the year and it is the parent’s principal place of abode.
maintains_household_of(Taxpayer, Parent, Year) :-
    household(Home),
    principal_abode(Parent, Home, Year),
    furnishes_cost(Taxpayer, Home, Year).

% §2(b)(1)(B): Head-of-Household status via parent
section2b1B_applies(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    unmarried(Taxpayer, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),
    parent_of(Parent, Taxpayer),
    maintains_household_of(Taxpayer, Parent, Year),
    deduction_for(Taxpayer, Parent, Year, section151).

/*****************************************************************
  Facts from the case
*****************************************************************/

% Marriage facts
spouse(bob, alice).
spouse_death(bob, alice, 2014).
remarried(bob, Year) :- fail.            % Bob never remarried

% Parental relationship
parent_of(charlie, bob).
father_of(charlie, bob).                 % Charlie is Bob's father

% Household facts 2004-2019
household(home1).
principal_abode(charlie, home1, Year) :-
    Year >= 2004,
    Year =< 2019.
furnishes_cost(bob, home1, Year) :-
    Year >= 2004,
    Year =< 2019.

% Deduction for Charlie allowed 2015-2019
deduction_for(bob, charlie, Year, section151) :-
    Year >= 2015,
    Year =< 2019.

/*****************************************************************
  Verification goal
*****************************************************************/
:- (  section2b1B_applies(bob, 2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* ------------- Statutory rules ------------- */

% Section 2(a) – surviving spouse
surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    spouse_died(Taxpayer, DeathYear),
    Diff is Year - DeathYear,
    Diff >= 1, Diff =< 2,               % within two years of spouse's death
    \+ remarried(Taxpayer, Year).       % has not remarried during the year

% Section 2(b)(1) – head-of-household requirements
section2b1_applies(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    not_married(Taxpayer, Year),                % taxpayer is not married
    \+ surviving_spouse(Taxpayer, Year),        % taxpayer is not a surviving spouse
    option_b_father_mother(Taxpayer, Year).     % satisfies §2(b)(1)(B)

% §2(b)(1)(B) – maintains household for father or mother (dependent)
option_b_father_mother(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    parent_of(Parent, Taxpayer),                % Parent is father or mother
    deduction151(Taxpayer, Parent, Year),       % taxpayer entitled to §151 deduction
    principal_abode(Parent, Household, Year),   % Parent’s principal abode is that household
    maintains_household(Taxpayer, Household, Year).

% Maintains household (over half of cost)
maintains_household(Taxpayer, Household, Year) :-
    over_half_cost(Taxpayer, Household, Year).

% A parent is a father or mother
parent_of(Parent, Child) :- father(Parent, Child).
parent_of(Parent, Child) :- mother(Parent, Child).

/* ------------- Marital-status rules ------------- */

% Not married at the close of the taxable year
not_married(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    \+ married_at_close(Taxpayer, Year).

% Married at close of year if spouse is alive, or spouse died during that year
married_at_close(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    current_spouse(Taxpayer, Spouse),
    alive(Spouse, Year).
married_at_close(Taxpayer, Year) :-
    spouse_died(Taxpayer, Year).

/* ------------- Facts from the case ------------- */

% Alice (Bob's spouse) died in 2014
spouse_died(bob, 2014).

% Bob never remarried
remarried(_, _) :- fail.

% Bob’s father Charlie
father(charlie, bob).

% Charlie and Bob share the same household in 2018
principal_abode(charlie, bob_home, 2018).

% Bob paid more than half the cost of that household in 2018
over_half_cost(bob, bob_home, 2018).

% Bob is entitled to a §151 deduction for Charlie in 2018
deduction151(bob, charlie, 2018).

% There is no living current spouse for Bob after Alice’s death
current_spouse(_, _) :- fail.
alive(_, _) :- fail.

/* ------------- Verification goal ------------- */
:- (  section2b1_applies(bob, 2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

nonresident_alien_in_year(alice,Year) :-

nonresident_alien_in_year(bob,_) :- fail.

% A taxpayer’s spouse died during the taxable year
spouse_died_during(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    spouse_of(Taxpayer,Spouse),
    died(Spouse,date(Year,_,_)).

% A taxpayer’s spouse is (or was at any time in the year) a non-resident alien
spouse_nonresident_alien_during(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    spouse_of(Taxpayer,Spouse),
    nonresident_alien(Spouse),
    % if the spouse was alive or died during that same year,
    ( \+ died(Spouse,_) ; died(Spouse,date(Year,_,_)) ).

applies_2b2c(Taxpayer,Year) :-
    spouse_died_during(Taxpayer,Year),

nonresident_alien(alice).               % Alice’s status
died(alice,date(2014,7,9)).             % Alice died during 2014

              to section 2(b)(3)(B) and section 152(d)(2)(H) ---------- */

    qualifies as the taxpayer's dependent *solely* by reason of
    living in the same household (and is not otherwise related).
    In the facts, we will state this directly.               */

is_dependent_under_152d2h(Taxpayer,Person,Year) :-
    declared_152d2h_dependent(Taxpayer,Person,Year).

    The taxpayer cannot be treated as a head-of-household
    “by reason of” an individual whose dependent status exists
    only under 152(d)(2)(H).  We say the limitation “applies”
    when the taxpayer’s only qualifying individual for HOH
    is such a dependent.                                     */

sec_2b3B_applies(Taxpayer,Person,Year) :-
    is_dependent_under_152d2h(Taxpayer,Person,Year),
    only_hoh_candidate(Taxpayer,Person,Year).

income(bob,Year,300000) :- between(2015,2019,Year).

declared_152d2h_dependent(bob,charlie,Year) :- between(2015,2019,Year).

employer(Person, Year) :-

excise_tax(Person, Year, Tax) :-

```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rule (abridged) ----------
   §3306(a)(1)(A)
   A person is an “employer” with respect to a calendar year
   if, during that year OR the preceding year, the person paid
   wages of $1,500 or more (other than certain domestic-service
   exclusions which are irrelevant to the present facts).
*/

/* ---------- FACTS FROM THE CASE ---------- */

wage_paid(alice, bob, 3200, 2017).
wage_paid(bob,   alice, 4500, 2017).
wage_paid(bob,   alice, 4500, 2018).

/* ---------- HELPER PREDICATES ---------- */

sum_list([],0).
sum_list([H|T],S) :- sum_list(T,S0), S is S0 + H.

/* total wages one person paid in a given calendar year */
wages_in_year(Payer, Year, Total) :-
    findall(Amount, wage_paid(Payer, _, Amount, Year), L),
    sum_list(L, Total).

/* ---------- STATUTORY TEST ---------- */

/* A person is an employer for Year if wages in Year ≥ 1500 */
employer(Person, Year) :-
    wages_in_year(Person, Year, W), W >= 1500.

/* …or if wages in the preceding year ≥ 1500 */
employer(Person, Year) :-
    PrevYear is Year - 1,
    wages_in_year(Person, PrevYear, W), W >= 1500.

/* ---------- VERIFICATION GOAL ---------- */

:- (  employer(alice, 2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* =========  Statutory rules relevant to §3306(a)(1)(B)  ========= */

/*  §3306(a)(1)(B) applies to an employer for a calendar year
    if the employer, on each of some 10 days during that calendar
    year (each day being in a different calendar week), employed
    at least one individual in employment for some portion of the
    day.                                                 	    */

%  To keep the model simple, we represent each day of employment
%  with a fact  employment(Employer,Employee,CalendarYear,CalendarWeek).
%  There is one fact per calendar week per employee if the employee
%  worked at least part of one day in that week.

%  an_employment_day/2  –– true when Employer had at least one
%  employment day in Week of Year.
an_employment_day(Employer,Year-Week) :-
    employment(Employer,_,Year,Week).

%  ten_distinct_weeks/2  –– true when Employer had employment
%  days in at least ten distinct calendar weeks of Year.
ten_distinct_weeks(Employer,Year) :-
    setof(Week, an_employment_day(Employer,Year-Week), Weeks),
    length(Weeks,L),
    L >= 10.

%  §3306(a)(1)(B) applies when the ten-week condition is met.
sec3306_a_1_B_applies(Employer,Year) :-
    ten_distinct_weeks(Employer,Year).


/* ======================  Case facts  ====================== */

%  The only information given is that “Alice has employed Bob on
%  various occasions during the year 2017.”  No details about
%  calendar weeks are supplied.  We therefore enter just one
%  illustrative fact –– not enough to reach the ten-week threshold.
employment(alice,bob,2017,1).


/* ==================  Verification goal  =================== */

%  The claim:  “Section 3306(a)(1)(B) applies to Alice for the year
%  2017.”  Translating:  sec3306_a_1_B_applies(alice,2017).

:- (  sec3306_a_1_B_applies(alice,2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')

reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules relevant to §3306(a)(1) ---------- */

/* wages(Payer, Payee, Amount, ServiceType, Year). 
   ServiceType is ‘domestic’ for domestic service (§3306(a)(3)),
   anything else is treated as ‘other’.                                      */

/* ---- Case facts ---- */
wages(alice, bob, 3200, domestic, 2017).
wages(bob,   alice, 4500, other,    2017).
wages(bob,   alice, 4500, other,    2018).   /* even if we record Bob’s 2018 payments, 
                                                this does not affect Alice’s status       */

/* ---- Helper: sum of elements in a list ---- */
sum_list([], 0).
sum_list([H|T], S) :- sum_list(T, S0), S is S0 + H.

/* ---- Total non-domestic wages paid by Employer in Year or preceding Year ---- */
total_non_domestic_wages(Employer, Year, Total) :-
        PrevYear is Year - 1,
        findall(Amount,
                ( wages(Employer, _, Amount, ServiceType, Y),
                  (Y = Year ; Y = PrevYear),
                  ServiceType \= domestic ),
                Amounts),
        sum_list(Amounts, Total).

/* ---- §3306(a)(1)(A): $1,500 non-domestic wage test ---- */
threshold_met_wages(Employer, Year) :-
        total_non_domestic_wages(Employer, Year, Total),
        Total >= 1500.

/* ---- §3306(a)(1)(B): 10-day employment test (no data -> cannot be proved) ---- */
threshold_met_employment(_, _) :- fail.

/* ---- Employer status under §3306(a)(1) ---- */
employer_a1(Employer, Year) :-
        threshold_met_wages(Employer, Year).
employer_a1(Employer, Year) :-
        threshold_met_employment(Employer, Year).

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */
:- (  employer_a1(alice, 2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

/* sum of all non-domestic wages paid by a person in a year */
wages_sum(Person, Year, Sum) :-
    findall(Amount,
            ( paid_wages(Person, _Payee, Amount, Year, ServiceType),
              ServiceType \= domestic ),
            Amounts),

/* ---------- Statutory rules (relevant extracts) ---------- */

/*  §3306(a)(2)(A)
    For agricultural labor, a person is an “employer” for a given calendar year Y
    if the person paid wages of $20,000 or more for agricultural labor
    either in year Y or in the preceding year Y-1.
*/
employer_agricultural(Person, Year) :-
    wages_for_year(Person, Year, agricultural, Sum),
    Sum >= 20000.
employer_agricultural(Person, Year) :-
    PrevYear is Year - 1,
    wages_for_year(Person, PrevYear, agricultural, Sum),
    Sum >= 20000.

/* Helper: total wages of a person for a service type during a calendar year */
wages_for_year(Person, Year, ServiceType, Total) :-
    findall(Amount,
            wage_record(Person, _Recipient, Amount, ServiceType, Year),
            Amounts),
    sum_list(Amounts, Total).


/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

wage_record(alice, bob,  3200, agricultural, 2017).

   but we include them for completeness */
wage_record(bob,   alice, 4520, agricultural, 2017).
wage_record(bob,   alice, 4520, agricultural, 2018).


/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  employer_agricultural(alice, 2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice has employed Bob from Jan 1st, 2011 to Oct 10, 2019. On Oct 10, 2019 Bob was diagnosed as disabled and retired. Alice paid Bob $12980 because she had to terminate their contract due to Bob's disability.
question: Section 3306(b)(10)(A) applies to the payment of $12980 that Alice made in 2019.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/*  Statutory rule: Section 3306(b)(10)(A)
    A payment is covered if –
      1. It is made by an employer to an employee (or the employee’s dependents),
      2. It is paid upon, or after, the termination of the employee’s
         employment relationship,
      3. The termination is because of the employee’s death OR retirement
         for disability.
*/

% ------- FACTS FROM THE CASE ---------------------------------------------

% the payment
payment(p1).
payment_employer(p1, alice).
payment_employee(p1, bob).
payment_amount(p1, 12980).
payment_date(p1, date(2019,10,10)).

% employment history and termination
employment_relationship_terminated_on(bob, date(2019,10,10)).
termination_reason(bob, retirement_for_disability).

% ------- AUXILIARY PREDICATE ---------------------------------------------

% same_or_after(Date1 , Date2)  – true when Date1 is the same day as,
% or later than, Date2.
same_or_after(date(Y1,M1,D1), date(Y2,M2,D2)) :-
    (  Y1  > Y2
    ;  Y1 == Y2, M1 >  M2
    ;  Y1 == Y2, M1 == M2, D1 >= D2
    ).

% ------- STATUTORY RULE ENCODING -----------------------------------------

sub3306b10a_applies(Payment) :-
    payment(Payment),
    payment_employee(Payment, Employee),
    employment_relationship_terminated_on(Employee, TermDate),
    payment_date(Payment, PayDate),
    same_or_after(PayDate, TermDate),
    termination_reason(Employee, Reason),
    ( Reason = retirement_for_disability
    ; Reason = death).

% ------- VERIFICATION GOAL ------------------------------------------------

:- (  sub3306b10a_applies(p1)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* =========  Statutory rules (extracts relevant to §3306(b)(10))  ========= */

/*  A payment is excluded from the definition of “wages” under
    §3306(b)(10) if BOTH of the following are true:

       (A) it is paid upon or after termination of the employment
           relationship because of death OR retirement for disability, and
       (B) it is paid under an employer-established plan that covers
           employees generally (or a class of employees).

    We model this with the predicate applies_3306_b_10/1              */

applies_3306_b_10(PayID) :-
        payment(PayID, _Employer, _Employee, _Date, _Amount,
                retirement_for_disability, Plan, _Year),
        plan_covers_employees_generally(Plan).

/*  A plan covers employees generally (or a class) if it is recorded as
    such with plan_property/2.                                           */

plan_covers_employees_generally(Plan) :-
        plan_property(Plan, covers_employees_generally).


/* ========================  Case facts  ========================= */

payment(p1, alice, bob, date(2019,10,10), 12980,
        retirement_for_disability, disability_plan_all, 2019).

/*  Alice’s disability plan is set up for all employees.               */
plan_property(disability_plan_all, covers_employees_generally).


/* =======================  Verification  ========================= */

:- (  applies_3306_b_10(p1)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

/* Statutory rules relevant to §3306(b)(11) */

% remuneration_for_agricultural_labor(Year)  –
%   true if someone received remuneration for agricultural labor in Year.
remuneration_for_agricultural_labor(Year) :-
    payment(_,_,Year,agricultural_labor,_,_).

% paid_non_cash(Year)  –
%   true if the remuneration in Year was paid in a medium other than cash.
paid_non_cash(Year) :-
    payment(_,_,Year,_,medium_non_cash,_).

% section3306b11_applies(Year)  –
%   §3306(b)(11) applies when the remuneration is for agricultural labor
%   AND it is paid in a medium other than cash.
section3306b11_applies(Year) :-
    remuneration_for_agricultural_labor(Year),

text: Alice employed Bob for agricultural labor from Feb 1st, 2011 to November 19th, 2019. On November 25th, Bob died from a heart attack. On January 20th, 2020, Alice paid Charlie, Bob's surviving spouse, Bob's outstanding wages of $1200.
question: Section 3306(b)(15) applies to the payment that Alice made to Charlie in 2020.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/****************************************************************
* Statutory rule (simplified)                                   *
* §3306(b)(15): Wages do NOT include “any payment made by an    *
* employer to a survivor or the estate of a former employee     *
* after the calendar year in which such employee died”.         *
****************************************************************/

% ---- helper: pull out the year component of a date ------------
year(date(Year, _Month, _Day), Year).

% ---- facts from the case --------------------------------------
employer(alice).

employee(bob).

death(bob, date(2019,11,25)).

survivor(bob, charlie).     % Charlie is Bob’s surviving spouse

% payment(Payer, Payee, ForEmployee, Amount, PaymentDate).
payment(alice, charlie, bob, 1200, date(2020,1,20)).

% ---- Rule: section_3306b15_applies/1 --------------------------
% True if §3306(b)(15) applies to a given payment.
section_3306b15_applies(payment(Payer, Payee, Emp, _Amt, PayDate)) :-
    employer(Payer),                % payment made by an employer
    death(Emp, DeathDate),          % employee has died
    year(DeathDate, DeathYear),
    year(PayDate, PayYear),
    PayYear > DeathYear,            % payment made AFTER calendar year of death
    ( survivor(Emp, Payee)          % payee is a survivor ...
    ; estate_of(Emp, Payee)         % ... or the estate (none in this case)
    ).

% convenience fact connecting the concrete payment term above
payment_id(p1, P) :- payment(alice, charlie, bob, 1200, D), P = payment(alice, charlie, bob, 1200, D).

% ---------------------------------------------------------------
% Query: does §3306(b)(15) apply to the payment Alice made?
:- payment_id(p1, P),
   (  section_3306b15_applies(P)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice has paid $45252 to Bob for work done in the year 2017. In 2017, Alice has also paid $9832 into a retirement fund for Bob, and paid $5322 into health insurance for Charlie, who is Alice's father and has retired in 2016.
question: Section 3306(b)(2)(A) applies to the payment Alice made to Bob for the year 2017.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/****************************************************************
 * Statutory rules distilled from §3306(b)(2)(A)
 *
 *  §3306(b)(2)(A) removes from the definition of “wages” any
 *  payment that is
 *     – made to (or on behalf of) an employee or the employee’s
 *       dependents,
 *     – made under an employer-established plan which covers
 *       employees generally (or a class of employees), and
 *     – made on account of sickness or accident disability.
 *
 *  We capture those conditions with a single predicate
 *         applies_3306b2A/3
 *  which is true when the statute applies to a payment.
 ****************************************************************/

% —————  FACTS FROM THE CASE  —————
% paid(Payer, Payee, Amount, Nature, Year).

paid(alice, bob,     45252, wages,            2017).
paid(alice, bob,      9832, retirement_fund,  2017).
paid(alice, charlie,  5322, health_insurance, 2017).

% —————  RULES  —————
% A payment is covered by §3306(b)(2)(A) only if it is a plan
% payment made because of sickness or accident disability.

% We record which kinds of payments are plan payments for
% sickness/accident disability.  None of Alice’s payments have
% that character, so there are no facts for plan_payment/4.

% plan_payment(Payer, Payee, Amount, Year).

% The statute applies if a qualifying plan payment exists.
applies_3306b2A(Payer, Payee, Year) :-
    plan_payment(Payer, Payee, _Amount, Year).

% —————  VERIFICATION GOAL  —————
% Does §3306(b)(2)(A) apply to the 2017 payment Alice made to Bob?
:- (  applies_3306b2A(alice, bob, 2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

/*****************************************************************
 Statutory rules – we encode only the condition that is relevant
 for §3306(b)(2)(C).  That paragraph excludes from “wages”:

   any payment an employer makes “for insurance … or into a fund”
   under a “plan or system established by an employer” which makes
   provision for (some or all of) his employees, where the payment
   is made “on account of … death.”

 For the exclusion to apply we therefore require BOTH
   1) the payment is for life-insurance (on account of death); and
   2) it is made under a qualifying plan or system.

 In Prolog we represent this with the predicate
        applies_3306_b_2c/3 (Employer, Employee, Year).
*****************************************************************/

%  §3306(b)(2)(C) – rule
applies_3306_b_2c(Employer, Employee, Year) :-
    payment(Employer, Employee, Year, life_insurance, _Amount),   % insurance → on account of death
    under_plan_or_system(Employer, Year).                         % made under qualifying plan


/*****************************************************************
 Facts taken from the case
*****************************************************************/
payment(alice, bob, 2017, wages,          45252).
payment(alice, bob, 2017, retirement_fund, 9832).
payment(alice, bob, 2017, life_insurance,  5322).

/*  The case file says nothing about Alice having any “plan or
    system … for her employees generally (or a class of them)”.
    Accordingly, we have NO fact ‘under_plan_or_system(alice,2017).’
*/


/*****************************************************************
 Verification goal
*****************************************************************/
:- (  applies_3306_b_2c(alice, bob, 2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

/* ------------- Statutory rules (simplified to what is needed here) ------------- */

% A cash payment from an employer to an employee in a given year
% cash_payment(Employer, Employee, Amount, Year).
% (case facts will be added below)

% Domestic service performed in the employer’s private home
% domestic_service(Employer, Employee).

% An employer who, in a calendar year, paid cash remuneration of
% at least $1,000 for domestic service is an “employer” for FUTA purposes
employer_domestic(Employer, Year) :-
    aggregate_all(sum(Amt),
                  (cash_payment(Employer, _Emp, Amt, Year),
                   domestic_service(Employer, _Emp)),
                  Total),
    Total >= 1000.

% Domestic service is “employment” under §3306(c)(2) only if the
% $1,000-in-cash threshold is met
employment(Employer, Employee, Year) :-
    domestic_service(Employer, Employee),
    employer_domestic(Employer, Year).

% “Wages” under §3306(b) are remuneration for “employment” unless an
% explicit exclusion applies.  (None of the listed exclusions apply here,
% so we simply require that the payment be cash and for employment.)
wages(Employer, Employee, Amount, Year) :-
    cash_payment(Employer, Employee, Amount, Year),
    employment(Employer, Employee, Year).

% The claim we want to verify: §3306(b) applies to the money paid
section3306b_applies(Employer, Employee, Amount, Year) :-
    wages(Employer, Employee, Amount, Year).

/* ----------------------------- Case facts ------------------------------------ */

cash_payment(alice, bob, 2325, 2018).
domestic_service(alice, bob).

/* ------------------------- Verification query -------------------------------- */

:- (  section3306b_applies(alice, bob, 2325, 2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

/* ---------- Statutory rules (relevant excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 152(c)(2): Relationship test for qualifying child */
relationship_152c2(Taxpayer, Child) :-
    % Child of the taxpayer (covers descendants as well in this simplified model)
    child_of(Child, Taxpayer).

/* Section 152(c)(3): Age requirements for qualifying child */
age_requirement_152c3(Taxpayer, Child, Year) :-
    % Directly use a fact that the age requirement is satisfied for the given year
    age_req_satisfied(Child, Taxpayer, Year).

/* Section 152(c)(1)(B): Same principal place of abode for more than one-half of the year */
same_abode_more_than_half_year(Taxpayer, Child, Year) :-
    principal_place_of_abode_same_more_than_half(Taxpayer, Child, Year).

/* Section 152(c)(1)(E): No disqualifying joint return with spouse */
% In this simplified model, this holds if there is no fact that the child filed
% a joint return with a spouse for that year (other than only for a claim of refund).
no_disqualifying_joint_return(Child, Year) :-
    \+ filed_joint_return_with_spouse_for_year(Child, Year).

/* Main definition: Section 152(c)(1) - Qualifying child */
qualifying_child_152c1(Taxpayer, Child, Year) :-
    relationship_152c2(Taxpayer, Child),
    same_abode_more_than_half_year(Taxpayer, Child, Year),
    age_requirement_152c3(Taxpayer, Child, Year),
    no_disqualifying_joint_return(Child, Year).

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

/* Alice has a son, Bob. */
child_of(bob, alice).

/* Bob satisfied section 152(c)(3) with Alice as the taxpayer for the years 2015 to 2020. */
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2015).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2016).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2017).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2018).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2019).
age_req_satisfied(bob, alice, 2020).

/* From September 1st, 2015 to November 3rd, 2019, Alice and Bob lived in the same home.
   This implies that for 2019 they had the same principal place of abode for more than
   one-half of that taxable year. */
principal_place_of_abode_same_more_than_half(alice, bob, 2019).

/* Bob married Charlie on October 23rd, 2018. */
married(bob, charlie, date(2018,10,23)).

/* No fact is given that Bob and Charlie filed a joint return for 2019.
   We model this by making any such query fail. */
filed_joint_return_with_spouse_for_year(_, _) :- fail.

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  qualifying_child_152c1(alice, bob, 2019)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
