reference_prolog:
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 152(a): "dependent" means a qualifying child or qualifying relative */

dependent_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifying_child_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year).

dependent_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    qualifying_relative_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year).

/* Section 152(d): qualifying relative */

/* Relationship test for qualifying relative under section 152(d)(2)
   (only relationships relevant to this case are modeled):
   - child or descendant of a child of the taxpayer
   - father or mother of the taxpayer, or an ancestor of either
*/

relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer) :-
    % Dependent is a child of Taxpayer (152(d)(2)(A): child)
    parent(Taxpayer, Dependent).

relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer) :-
    % Dependent is a descendant of a child of Taxpayer (grandchild, etc.)
    ancestor(Taxpayer, Dependent).

relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer) :-
    % Dependent is father or mother of Taxpayer (152(d)(2)(C))
    parent(Dependent, Taxpayer).

relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer) :-
    % Dependent is an ancestor of Taxpayer (e.g., grandparent)
    ancestor(Dependent, Taxpayer).

/* Ancestor relation (transitive closure of parent) */

ancestor(Ancestor, Descendant) :-
    parent(Ancestor, Descendant).

ancestor(Ancestor, Descendant) :-
    parent(Ancestor, Intermediate),
    ancestor(Intermediate, Descendant).

/* Income test for qualifying relative under section 152(d)(1)(B):
   "has no income" in the calendar year
*/

has_no_income(Person, Year) :-
    income(Person, Year, 0).

/* Full qualifying relative test for a given year (152(d)(1)):
   - bears a qualifying-relative relationship
   - has no income
   - is not a qualifying child of any taxpayer
*/

qualifying_relative_of(Dependent, Taxpayer, Year) :-
    relationship_qr(Dependent, Taxpayer),
    has_no_income(Dependent, Year),
    \+ qualifying_child_of(Dependent, _, Year).

/* Section 152(b)(1): Dependents ineligible to have dependents.
   It "applies" to an individual in a given year if that individual
   is a dependent of some taxpayer for a taxable year beginning in
   that calendar year.
*/

section_152b1_applies(Person, Year) :-
    dependent_of(Person, _, Year).



/* ---------- Facts for the case (year 2015) ---------- */

/* Family relationships */
parent(alice, bob).
parent(bob, charlie).

/* Qualifying child facts as given in the case (Section 152(c)(1) satisfied) */
qualifying_child_of(bob, alice, 2015).
qualifying_child_of(charlie, bob, 2015).

/* Incomes for 2015 */
income(alice, 2015, 504598).
income(bob, 2015, 0).



/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */
/* Claim: Section 152(b)(1) applies to Alice for the year 2015. */

:- (  section_152b1_applies(alice, 2015)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.

/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpt) ---------- */

/* §3306(c)(2): Domestic service in a private home, local college club, or local chapter of a college fraternity or sorority is excluded from "employment"
   unless performed for a person who paid cash remuneration of $1,000 or more to individuals employed in such domestic service in the calendar year
   or the preceding calendar year. */

statute_3306c2_excluded_from_employment(Employer, Year) :-
    % The service qualifies as "domestic service in a private home" (facts will establish that).
    domestic_service_in_private_home(Employer, Year),

    paid_wages_to_alice_bob(Year, Amount),
    Employer = alice.

/* ---------- Derivation of whether §3306(c)(2) applies (i.e., excludes the service from "employment") ---------- */

text: Alice has paid wages of $300 to Bob for domestic service in her private home from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017.

reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules we need ---------- */

%  §3306(c)(2) – domestic service in a private home is
%  EXCLUDED from “employment” UNLESS the employer paid
%  cash remuneration of $1,000 or more for such service
%  in the current or the preceding calendar year.

% helper – sum of all domestic‐service wages an employer paid in a year
total_domestic_wages(Employer,Year,Total) :-
    findall(Amount, wages_domestic(Employer,_,Year,Amount), List),
    sum_list(List,Total).

% did the employer reach the $1,000 threshold in a given year?
cash_domestic_wages_ge_1000(Employer,Year) :-
    total_domestic_wages(Employer,Year,Total),
    Total >= 1000.

% the threshold is satisfied in either the current or the preceding year
threshold_met(Employer,Year) :-
       cash_domestic_wages_ge_1000(Employer,Year)
    ;  PrevYear is Year - 1,
       cash_domestic_wages_ge_1000(Employer,PrevYear).

/* 3306(c)(2) applies (i.e., the exclusion governs) when:
     1) the service is domestic service in a private home, AND
     2) the $1,000 threshold is NOT met in either the current
        or the preceding calendar year.                               */
applies_3306c2(Employer,Employee,Year) :-
    domestic_service_private_home(Employer,Employee,Year),
    \+ threshold_met(Employer,Year).


/* ---------- Case-specific facts ---------- */

% Bob performed domestic service in Alice’s private home in 2017.
domestic_service_private_home(alice,bob,2017).

% Alice paid Bob $300 cash wages for that service in 2017.
wages_domestic(alice,bob,2017,300).

% Alice paid no other domestic-service wages in 2016 or 2017.
% (absence of facts implies zero wages for those years).


/* ---------- Verification query ---------- */

:- (  applies_3306c2(alice,bob,2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpt) ---------- */

/*  §3306(c)(5)
    (A) Service performed by an individual in the employ of
        his son, daughter, or spouse.
    (B) Service performed by a child under the age of 21
        in the employ of his father or mother.
*/

/* Rule: 3306(c)(5) applies if either subdivision (A) or (B) is satisfied. */
applies_3306c5(Employer,Employee,Year) :-
    employs(Employer,Employee,Year),
    subdivision_5A(Employer,Employee).

applies_3306c5(Employer,Employee,Year) :-
    employs(Employer,Employee,Year),
    subdivision_5B(Employer,Employee).

/* 5(A): employee works for his son, daughter, or spouse
         → employer is child or spouse of employee            */
subdivision_5A(Employer,Employee) :-
    child_of(Employer,Employee).        % Employer is a child of employee
subdivision_5A(Employer,Employee) :-
    spouse_of(Employer,Employee).       % Employer is a spouse of employee

/* 5(B): employee is a child (<21) working for father/mother
         → employer is parent of employee AND employee <21   */
subdivision_5B(Employer,Employee) :-
    parent_of(Employer,Employee),
    age(Employee,Age),
    Age < 21.

/* ---------- Background relationship rules ---------- */
parent_of(P,C)  :- child_of(C,P).
child_of(C,P)   :- parent_of(P,C).

/* Spousal relationship is symmetrical */
spouse_of(X,Y)  :- spouse_pair(X,Y).
spouse_of(X,Y)  :- spouse_pair(Y,X).

/* ---------- Case facts ---------- */
employs(alice,bob,2017).

/* Family relationship: Alice and Bob are siblings only */
sibling_of(alice,bob).
sibling_of(bob,alice).

/* No parent/child or spousal relationships stated */
% (there are intentionally no parent_of/2, child_of/2, or spouse_pair/2 facts)

/* Bob’s age (not under 21) */
age(bob,30).

/* ---------- Verification ---------- */
:- (  applies_3306c5(alice,bob,2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice has paid $3200 to Bob for work done from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017, in Caracas, Venezuela. Alice is a citizen of Venezuela, and Bob is an American citizen.
question: Section 3306(c)(B) applies to Alice employing Bob for the year 2017.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules (excerpted and simplified) ---------- */

/* 3306(c)(B) applies when:
      – Service is performed outside the United States,
      – The employee is a U.S. citizen,
      – The employer is an “American employer” (here modelled as a U.S. citizen)  */

applies_3306cB(Employer,Employee,Year) :-
        service_outside_us(Employer,Employee,Year),
        citizen(Employee,usa),
        american_employer(Employer).

/* An employer is an American employer if the employer is a U.S. citizen.  */
american_employer(E) :- citizen(E,usa).

/* ---------- Case-specific facts ---------- */

citizen(alice,venezuela).
citizen(bob,usa).

/* Alice paid Bob for work done in Caracas, Venezuela, in 2017.            */
service_outside_us(alice,bob,2017).

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */

:- (  applies_3306cB(alice,bob,2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice has paid $3200 to Bob for work done from Feb 1st, 2017 to Sep 2nd, 2017, in Caracas, Venezuela. Bob is an American citizen and Alice is an American employer.
question: Section 3306(c)(B) applies to Alice employing Bob for the year 2017.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ========= Statutory rules we need for this claim ========= */

% 3306(c)(B) – Employment includes
%   “service performed outside the United States … by a citizen of the
%    United States as an employee of an American employer”
%   UNLESS an explicit exclusion (1,2,5,6,7,10,11,13,16,21) applies.

% Basic predicates describing a service engagement
service(Employer,Employee,Year,Country).
american_employer(Employer).
us_citizen(Person).

outside_us(Country) :- Country \= united_states.

% Exclusion situations that would block 3306(c)(B).
% (We have no evidence of any exclusion in the case, so they are left empty;
%  if an exclusion fact were present, the rule below would deactivate 3306(c)(B).)
excluded_agricultural(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_domestic_service(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_family_service(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_us_government(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_state_government(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_school_student_or_spouse(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_hospital_patient(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_foreign_government(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_student_nurse(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_international_organization(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).
excluded_penal_institution(_Employer,_Employee,_Year).

% “Section 3306(c)(B) applies” when all positive conditions hold
% and no exclusion holds.
section_3306_c_b_applies(Employer,Employee,Year) :-
        service(Employer,Employee,Year,Country),
        outside_us(Country),
        us_citizen(Employee),
        american_employer(Employer),
        \+ excluded_agricultural(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_domestic_service(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_family_service(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_us_government(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_state_government(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_school_student_or_spouse(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_hospital_patient(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_foreign_government(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_student_nurse(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_international_organization(Employer,Employee,Year),
        \+ excluded_penal_institution(Employer,Employee,Year).

/* ========= Facts from the case ========= */

american_employer(alice).
us_citizen(bob).

% Bob worked for Alice from Feb 1 2017 to Sep 2 2017 in Caracas, Venezuela.
% That entire period is in calendar year 2017 and is outside the US.
service(alice,bob,2017,caracas_venezuela).

/* ========= Verification goal ========= */

:- (  section_3306_c_b_applies(alice,bob,2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $33200. She is allowed a deduction under section 63(c) of $2000 and deductions of $4000 under section 151 for the year 2017.
question: Under section 63(a), Alice's taxable income in 2017 is equal to $31200.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/************************************************************************
 * Rules derived from the statutes
 *
 *  §63(a):  Taxable income  =  Gross income  –  (all deductions allowed
 *           by this chapter other than the standard deduction).
 *
 *  In the present case, the only deduction of this kind is the personal
 *  exemption allowed under §151.  The standard deduction allowed under
 *  §63(c) is NOT subtracted when applying §63(a).
 ************************************************************************/

% --------- Facts from the case ---------------------------------------

gross_income(alice, 2017, 33200).
standard_deduction(alice, 2017, 2000).
personal_exemption(alice, 2017, 4000).

% --------- Statutory computation under §63(a) ------------------------

taxable_income_63a(Person, Year, TaxableIncome) :-
    gross_income(Person, Year, Gross),
    personal_exemption(Person, Year, PE),          % §151 deduction
    % Standard deduction is NOT taken under §63(a)
    TaxableIncome is Gross - PE.

% --------- The claim to be verified ----------------------------------

claim_taxable_income(alice, 2017, 31200).

% --------- Verification query ----------------------------------------

:- (  taxable_income_63a(alice, 2017, TI),
      claim_taxable_income(alice, 2017, TI)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* Statutory rules – fragment needed for this problem */

/*  Section 63(a):  For a taxpayer who itemizes, 
    taxable income = gross income – (all deductions other than the standard deduction) */

taxable_income(Person, TaxableIncome) :-
    itemizes(Person),                         % taxpayer chose to itemize
    gross_income(Person, Gross),              % gross income
    personal_exemption_deduction(Person, PE), % §151 deduction
    itemized_deduction(Person, ID),           % itemized deductions (§63(d))
    TotalDeductions is PE + ID,               % total deductions allowed under §63(a)
    TaxableIncome is Gross - TotalDeductions. % compute taxable income


/* Facts from the case */

gross_income(alice, 33200).
personal_exemption_deduction(alice, 2000).
itemized_deduction(alice, 4252).
itemizes(alice).

/* The claim to be verified */

claimed_taxable_income(alice, 26948).


/* Verification */

:- (  taxable_income(alice, TI),
      claimed_taxable_income(alice, TI)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* ------------- Statutory rules (excerpted and simplified) ------------- */

% filing_status rules taken from §63(c)(2)
%  - joint return (or surviving spouse) : 200% × $3,000  = $6,000
%  - head-of-household                 : $4,400
%  - all other individuals             : $3,000

basic_standard_deduction(TP,Year,6000) :- joint_return(TP,Year).
basic_standard_deduction(TP,Year,4400) :- head_of_household(TP,Year).
basic_standard_deduction(TP,Year,3000) :-
    \+ joint_return(TP,Year),
    \+ head_of_household(TP,Year).

/* ------------- Auxiliary status predicates ------------- */

% A taxpayer files a joint return if that taxpayer and the spouse file jointly.
joint_return(TP,Year) :-
    filed_joint_return(TP,_,Year).
joint_return(TP,Year) :-
    filed_joint_return(_,TP,Year).

% Head-of-household requires being unmarried and meeting several other tests.
% In this case none of those tests are satisfied, so the predicate is left
% intentionally undefined for the given facts; Prolog will therefore fail
% for Alice.

head_of_household(_,_) :- fail.   % no facts make anyone HOH in this case

/* ------------- Facts from the problem ------------- */

taxpayer(alice).
taxpayer(bob).

% Alice and Bob married on 3 Feb 2017
married(alice,bob,date(2017,2,3)).

% They file a joint return for 2017
filed_joint_return(alice,bob,2017).

/* ------------- Verification of the claim ------------- */

:-  (  basic_standard_deduction(alice,2017,4400)
    -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
    ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
    ).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $33200. Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017. Alice and Bob file separate returns.
question: Under section 63(c)(2)(C), Alice's basic standard deduction in 2017 is equal to $3000.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules (subset of §63(c) needed for this case) ---------- */

/* basic_standard_deduction(Person, Year, Amount)
   determines the basic standard deduction Amount allowed to Person for Year   */

% §63(c)(7): new dollar amounts apply only for 2018-2025 inclusive
uses_new_amounts(Year) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.

% Dollar amounts under §63(c)(2) when §63(c)(7) does NOT apply (e.g. 2017)
std_amount(joint_or_surviving_spouse,   6000).   % 200 % of $3,000
std_amount(head_of_household,           4400).   % $4,400
std_amount(other,                       3000).   % $3,000

% Dollar amounts when §63(c)(7) DOES apply (2018-2025)
std_amount_new(joint_or_surviving_spouse, 24000).  % 200 % of $12,000
std_amount_new(head_of_household,        18000).  % $18,000
std_amount_new(other,                    12000).  % $12,000

% Married-filing-separate is NOT a joint return, NOT head-of-household, so it falls
% in the “other” category of §63(c)(2)(C)
category(Person, Year, other) :-
    filing_status(Person, married_filing_separate),
    YearYear = Year.  % dummy to avoid unused variable warning

% Compute the basic standard deduction given the category and year
basic_standard_deduction(Person, Year, Amount) :-
    category(Person, Year, Cat),
    (   uses_new_amounts(Year)
    ->  std_amount_new(Cat, Amount)
    ;   std_amount(Cat, Amount)
    ).

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

person(alice).
year(2017).

% Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3 2017 and file separate returns.
married(alice,bob,2017).
filing_status(alice, married_filing_separate).
% No fact states that either spouse itemizes deductions, so §63(c)(6)(A) does not bar the standard deduction.

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */

:- (  basic_standard_deduction(alice, 2017, 3000)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
additional_standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, Total) :-
```

```prolog
% -------- Statutory rules (encoded) ----------
% Section 63(c)(3): the additional standard deduction equals
% the sum of each “additional amount” to which the taxpayer is
% entitled under subsection (f).
additional_standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Total) :-
    findall(Amount, additional_amount(Taxpayer, Amount), List),
    sum_list(List, Total).

% helper to sum a list of numbers
sum_list([], 0).
sum_list([H|T], S) :-
    sum_list(T, R),
    S is H + R.

% -------- Case-specific facts ----------
% Alice is entitled to two additional amounts of $600 each
% (one for herself, one for her spouse Bob).
additional_amount(alice, 600).   % 63(f)(1)(A) – for Alice herself
additional_amount(alice, 600).   % 63(f)(1)(B) – for spouse Bob

% -------- Verification of the claim ----------
:- (  additional_standard_deduction(alice, 1200)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* Section 63(c)(5) applies to an individual if (and only if)
   some other taxpayer is allowed a deduction for that individual
   under §151 for the year.                                    */

section63c5_applies(Person, Year) :-
        deduction_allowed(OtherTaxpayer, Person, Year),
        OtherTaxpayer \= Person.

   deduction for Bob.  Hence no deduction_allowed/3 fact is asserted
   for (alice, bob, 2017).  Likewise, no other taxpayer is stated to
   have such a deduction.  Under the closed-world assumption, the
   absence of a deduction_allowed/3 fact means none exists.          */
```

text: In 2017, Alice was paid $33200. Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017. Alice is entitled to a deduction for Bob under section 151(b). Bob had no gross income in 2017.
question: Under section 63(c)(5), Bob's basic standard deduction in 2017 is equal to at most $500.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules (relevant excerpts) ---------- */

/* Section 63(c)(5)  – limitation on the basic standard deduction
   If a deduction under section 151 is allowable to another taxpayer
   with respect to an individual, that individual’s basic standard
   deduction may not exceed the greater of:
        A) $500
        B) $250 + the individual’s earned income.
*/
basic_standard_deduction_limit(Person, Limit) :-
        deduction_under_151_allowed_to_another(Person),
        earned_income(Person, EarnedIncome),
        Temp is 250 + EarnedIncome,
        ( Temp > 500 -> Limit = Temp
        ;               Limit = 500 ).

/* ---------- Case-specific facts ---------- */

deduction_under_151_allowed_to_another(bob).
earned_income(bob, 0).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */
/* The claim: Under §63(c)(5), Bob's basic standard deduction in 2017
   is equal to at most $500.  This is true iff the computed limit for
   Bob is ≤ 500.
*/
:- (  basic_standard_deduction_limit(bob, Limit),
      Limit =< 500
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
% Statutory rules (relevant excerpts)

% A married individual for a given year
married_individual(Person,Year) :-
    married(Person,_,Year).

% Person filed a joint return for the year
filed_joint(Person,Year) :-
    joint_return(Year,Person,_);
    joint_return(Year,_,Person).

% Person filed a separate return for the year
separate_return(Person,Year) :-
    married_individual(Person,Year),
    \+ filed_joint(Person,Year).

% Section 63(c)(6)(A) applies
applies_63c6a(Person,Year) :-
    married_individual(Person,Year),
    separate_return(Person,Year),
    ( itemizes(Person,Year)
    ; married(Person,Spouse,Year), itemizes(Spouse,Year)
    ).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Facts from the case

% Alice and Bob have been married since Feb 3rd, 2017.
married(alice,bob,2017).
married(bob,alice,2017).         % make marriage symmetric

% Alice and Bob file a joint return for 2017.
joint_return(2017,alice,bob).

% (No information that either spouse itemizes deductions.)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------

% Query to verify the claim
:- (  applies_63c6a(alice,2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
itemizes(bob,2017).

% -------- Verification goal --------
```

```prolog
   section63_c_6_d_applies(Entity,Year).                             */
```

```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules we need ---------- */

/* years for which the temporary higher standard deduction applies */
tcja_year(Year) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.

/* basic standard deduction amounts under §63(c)(2) as modified by §63(c)(7) */
basic_standard_deduction_amount(Year, joint_or_surviving_spouse, 24000) :-
    tcja_year(Year).
basic_standard_deduction_amount(Year, head_of_household, 18000) :-
    tcja_year(Year).
basic_standard_deduction_amount(Year, other, 12000) :-
    tcja_year(Year).

/* §63(c)(6)(A) – married filing separately with the other spouse itemizing  ⇒  zero   */
standard_deduction_limited_to_zero(Year, Taxpayer) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, married_separate),
    spouse_itemizes_deductions(Taxpayer, Year).   /* will be false unless stated */

/* general rule: basic standard deduction for a taxpayer */
basic_standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, Amount) :-
    \+ standard_deduction_limited_to_zero(Year, Taxpayer),   % no forced-zero rule
    filing_category(Taxpayer, Year, Category),
    basic_standard_deduction_amount(Year, Category, Amount).

basic_standard_deduction(Taxpayer, Year, 0) :-
    standard_deduction_limited_to_zero(Year, Taxpayer).

/* determine filing-category from filing-status */
filing_category(Taxpayer, _Year, joint_or_surviving_spouse) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, joint).
filing_category(Taxpayer, _Year, joint_or_surviving_spouse) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, surviving_spouse).
filing_category(Taxpayer, _Year, head_of_household) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, head_of_household).
filing_category(Taxpayer, _Year, other) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, married_separate).
filing_category(Taxpayer, _Year, other) :-
    filing_status(Taxpayer, single).

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

tax_year(2019).

% marriage facts
married(alice,bob,date(2019,2,3)).

% filing status facts
filing_status(alice, married_separate).

% No information that Bob itemized deductions; assume he did not.
spouse_itemizes_deductions(_Taxpayer, _Year) :- fail.

/* ---------- Claim to be verified ---------- */

claimed_basic_standard_deduction(alice, 2019, 3000).

/* ---------- Verification ---------- */

:- tax_year(Y),
   basic_standard_deduction(alice, Y, Actual),
   claimed_basic_standard_deduction(alice, Y, Claimed),
   (  Actual =:= Claimed
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules ---------- */

% subparagraph (C) amount (section 63(c)(2)(C)),
% with the temporary increase provided by section 63(c)(7)(ii)
subC_amount(Year, 12000) :- Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.   % special rule years
subC_amount(Year, 3000)  :- (Year < 2018 ; Year > 2025).  % otherwise

% basic standard deduction, section 63(c)(2),
% incorporating the special amounts of section 63(c)(7)
basic_standard_deduction(Tp, Year, Amount) :-
    filing_status(Tp, married_joint),               % 63(c)(2)(A)
    subC_amount(Year, SubC),
    Amount is 2 * SubC.

basic_standard_deduction(Tp, Year, Amount) :-
    filing_status(Tp, surviving_spouse),            % 63(c)(2)(A)
    subC_amount(Year, SubC),
    Amount is 2 * SubC.

basic_standard_deduction(Tp, Year, 18000) :-        % 63(c)(2)(B) with 63(c)(7)(i)
    filing_status(Tp, head_of_household),
    Year >= 2018, Year =< 2025.

basic_standard_deduction(Tp, Year, 4400) :-         % 63(c)(2)(B) before/after special years
    filing_status(Tp, head_of_household),
    (Year < 2018 ; Year > 2025).

% “Any other case” – includes a married individual filing separately
basic_standard_deduction(Tp, Year, Amount) :-       % 63(c)(2)(C)
    filing_status(Tp, other),
    subC_amount(Year, Amount).

/* ---------- Case facts ---------- */

tax_year(2019).
person(alice).
person(bob).

% Alice and Bob have been married since 3 Feb 2019
married(alice, bob, 2019).

% Alice files a separate return for 2019
filing_status(alice, married_separate).

% Treat “married_separate” as “other” for 63(c)(2)(C)
filing_status(Tp, other) :- filing_status(Tp, married_separate).

/* ---------- Verification of the claim ---------- */

:- (  basic_standard_deduction(alice, 2019, 12000)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* =========  Statutory rules we need  ========= */

/*  Section 63(d) – “itemized deductions” are all deductions 
    allowed under this chapter OTHER THAN
      (1) deductions used to arrive at AGI (“above-the-line”), and
      (2) the personal-exemption deduction of §151.
*/
excluded_type(above_line).
excluded_type(personal_exemption).

/*  A deduction falls under §63(d) (i.e., is an “itemized deduction”)
    when it is a deduction and its type is not excluded.            */
falls_under_63d(Deduction) :-
    deduction(Deduction),

    \+ excluded_type(Type).

    deductions are classic below-the-line (itemized) deductions,

deduction(alice_2017_donation).
deduction_type(alice_2017_donation,charitable_contribution).

   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')

   ).
```

```prolog
% A taxpayer is entitled to the additional aged amount
% if the taxpayer has attained age 65 before the close of the taxable year.

% Determine whether a person has reached age 65 before the end of a tax year
reached_65(Person, Year) :-
    birthdate(Person, date(BirthYear, BirthMonth, BirthDay)),
    % Calculate tentative age simply by year difference
    Age is Year - BirthYear,
    Age  > 65                                             % already older than 65
    ;
    ( Age =:= 65,                                         % turns 65 during the year
      % Birthday must be on or before 12-31 of that year, which is always true
      % because the birthday occurs within the calendar year by definition
      true).

% Section 63(f)(1)(A) applies when the taxpayer reached age 65
section63f1a_applies(Person, Year) :-
    reached_65(Person, Year).

% Verification goal

   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')

   ).
```

```prolog
    if BOTH of the following are true:

           for that spouse for the same year.                                      */

applies_section63_f_1_b(Taxpayer,Spouse,Year) :-

age(Person,Year,Age) :-
```

```prolog
% Statutory rules (relevant excerpts)

% A spouse satisfies section 63(f)(1)(B) for a taxpayer in a given year
% if: (1) they are married that year,
%     (2) the spouse has attained age 65 before the close of that year, and
%     (3) an additional exemption for the spouse is allowable to the taxpayer under §151(b).

section63f1b_applies_to(Taxpayer, Spouse, Year) :-
    married(Taxpayer, Spouse, Year),
    attained_age(Spouse, 65, Year),
    exemption_allowed_for_spouse(Taxpayer, Spouse, Year).

% Helper rule – age 65 attained if (Year - BirthYear) ≥ 65.
attained_age(Person, AgeNeeded, Year) :-
    birth(Person, date(BirthYear, _Month, _Day)),
    Age is Year - BirthYear,
    Age >= AgeNeeded.

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% Case-specific facts for 2017

% Marriage
married(bob, alice, 2017).
married(alice, bob, 2017).

% Birthdates
birth(alice, date(1950, 3, 2)).
birth(bob,   date(1955, 3, 3)).

% §151(b) additional exemption for spouse Alice is allowable to Bob
exemption_allowed_for_spouse(bob, alice, 2017).

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% Verification goal: does §63(f)(1)(B) apply to Bob with Alice in 2017?

:- (  section63f1b_applies_to(bob, alice, 2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
section63_f_2_A_applies(Taxpayer, Year) :-

/* Deriving blindness for a particular year */

% If a person has been blind since (or before) a given year, the person
% is blind for that year.
blind(Person, Year) :-
    blind_since(Person, SinceYear),
    SinceYear =< Year.

/* Case facts */

% Alice has been blind since 2015.
blind_since(alice, 2015).

% There is no fact stating that Bob is blind.

/* Verification */

:- (  section63_f_2_A_applies(bob, 2017)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
applies_63_f_2_a(Person,Year) :-

taxable_year(2017).
```

```prolog
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% Statutory rules – section 63(f)
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% A person is aged (within the meaning of §63(f)(1)) for a tax
% year if he or she has attained age 65 before the close of that
% taxable year.
aged(Person, Year) :-
    birth(Person, BirthYear, _, _),
    Year - BirthYear >= 65.

% Default additional amount under §63(f)(1): $600 for the aged.
base_additional_amount(600).

% Higher amount for certain unmarried individuals – §63(f)(3)
higher_amount(750).

% A person is unmarried in a year when we have no evidence of
% marriage or surviving-spouse status (closed-world assumption).
unmarried(Person, Year) :-
    \+ married(Person, Year),
    \+ surviving_spouse(Person, Year).

% Additional standard deduction amount for a taxpayer.
additional_standard_deduction(Person, Year, Amount) :-
    aged(Person, Year),
    (   unmarried(Person, Year)
    ->  higher_amount(Amount)
    ;   base_additional_amount(Amount)
    ).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% Facts supplied by the problem
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

birth(alice, 1950, 3, 2).
birth(bob,   1955, 3, 3).

% Income paid to Alice in 2017 – not directly relevant here.
income(alice, 2017, 33200).

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% Verification goal: does Alice’s additional standard
% deduction for 2017 equal $600?
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
:- (  additional_standard_deduction(alice, 2017, 600)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* ---------- statutory rules relevant to the claim ---------- */

/* applicable amount under §68(b)(1) */
applicable_amount(head_of_household, 275000).
applicable_amount(joint_or_surviving_spouse, 300000).
applicable_amount(single, 250000).
applicable_amount(married_separate, 150000).

/* overall limitation on itemized deductions (§68(a))            */
/* Reduction = lesser of                                          */
/*    3% * (AGI - applicable_amount) , or                         */
/*    80% * itemized_deductions                                   */
reduction(Reduction) :-
    agi(AGI),
    filing_status(Status),
    applicable_amount(Status, AA),
    itemized_deductions(Itemized),
    Excess is max(AGI - AA, 0),        % excess of AGI over threshold
    Red1  is 0.03 * Excess,            % 3 percent of excess
    Red2  is 0.80 * Itemized,          % 80 percent of itemized deductions
    min(Red1, Red2, Reduction).

min(A,B,A) :- A =< B, !.
min(_,B,B).

/* ---------- facts from the case ---------- */
tax_year(2016).
agi(267192).
filing_status(head_of_household).
itemized_deductions(60000).

/* ---------- verify the claim ---------- */
:- (  reduction(R), round(R) =:= 306
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules ---------- */

% Applicable amount under §68(b)(1)
applicable_amount(surviving_spouse , 300000).
applicable_amount(joint_return     , 300000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household, 275000).
applicable_amount(single           , 250000).
applicable_amount(married_separate , 150000).   % 1/2 of 300,000

% §68(f) – section does not apply for years 2018-2025
section68_applies(Year) :-
    \+ (Year >= 2018, Year < 2026).

% §68(a) reduction calculation
reduction(AGI, Itemized, Status, RoundedReduction) :-
    applicable_amount(Status, Limit),
    Excess is max(0, AGI - Limit),
    Red1  is 0.03 * Excess,          % §68(a)(1)
    Red2  is 0.80 * Itemized,        % §68(a)(2)
    MinRed is min(Red1, Red2),
    round(MinRed, RoundedReduction).

/* ---------- Case facts ---------- */

taxpayer(alice).
year(alice, 2016).
status(alice, surviving_spouse).
agi(alice, 310192).
itemized_deductions(alice, 60000).

/* ---------- Claim verification ---------- */

claim_holds :-
    taxpayer(alice),
    year(alice, Y),           section68_applies(Y),
    agi(alice, AGI),
    itemized_deductions(alice, Itemized),
    status(alice, Status),
    reduction(AGI, Itemized, Status, 306).

/* ---------- Output result ---------- */

:- (  claim_holds
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: In 2016, Alice's income was $295192. Alice is a surviving spouse for the year 2016. Alice is allowed itemized deductions of $60000 under section 63.
question: Section 68(a)(2) prescribes a reduction of Alice's itemized deductions for the year 2016 by $47000.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rules relevant to §68 ---------- */

/* applicable amount under §68(b)(1) */
applicable_amount(surviving_spouse, 300000).
applicable_amount(head_of_household, 275000).
applicable_amount(unmarried, 250000).
applicable_amount(married_filing_separately, 150000).   /* half of 300,000 */

/* §68(f) – section does NOT apply for years 2018-2025 */
section68_suspended(Year) :-
    Year >= 2018,
    Year =< 2025.

/* overall limitation calculation */
section68_reduction(Year, Status, AGI, Itemized, Reduction) :-
    (  section68_suspended(Year)
    -> Reduction = 0                                    % no limitation in suspension years
    ;  applicable_amount(Status, Limit),
       Excess is AGI - Limit,
       (  Excess =< 0
       -> Reduction = 0                                 % no excess → no reduction
       ;  R1 is 0.03 * Excess,                          % 3% of excess AGI
          R2 is 0.80 * Itemized,                        % 80% of itemized deductions
          ( R1 =< R2 -> Reduction = R1 ; Reduction = R2 )
       )
    ).

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */
tax_year(2016).
status(surviving_spouse).
adjusted_gross_income(295192).
itemized_deductions(60000).

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */
:- tax_year(Y),
   status(S),
   adjusted_gross_income(AGI),
   itemized_deductions(Itemized),
   section68_reduction(Y, S, AGI, Itemized, Reduction),
   (  Reduction =:= 47000
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
status(Person, unmarried) :-
    \+ status(Person, married),
    \+ status(Person, surviving_spouse),
    \+ status(Person, head_of_household).

applicable_amount(Person, _Year, 300000) :-
    status(Person, married_joint_return) ; status(Person, surviving_spouse).

applicable_amount(Person, _Year, 275000) :-
    status(Person, head_of_household).

applicable_amount(Person, _Year, 250000) :-
```

```prolog
legally_separated(bob, alice, date(2017,9,16)).

:- (  section7703a2_applies(alice, 2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')

:- halt.
```

```prolog
/* ----------  Statutory rules (simplified to what is needed here) ---------- */

% section7703b1_applies(Taxpayer,Year)  holds if all elements of §7703(b)(1) are met
section7703b1_applies(T,Year) :-
        married(T,Year),                         % “an individual who is married”
        separate_return(T,Year),                 % “who files a separate return”
        maintains_home_for_child(T,Child,Year),  % “maintains as his home a household …”
        entitled_to_dep_deduction(T,Child,Year). % “with respect to whom such individual is entitled to a deduction”

/* helper predicate saying the taxpayer maintains the home which is the
   child’s principal place of abode for more than half the year              */
maintains_home_for_child(T,Child,Year) :-
        maintains_home(T,Year),
        principal_abode(Child,T,Year),
        more_than_half_year(Child,T,Year).

/* ----------  Facts from the case ---------- */

% marriage still in force during 2018
married(alice,2018).

% Alice files a separate return
separate_return(alice,2018).

% Alice has maintained the home since 16-Sep-2017
maintains_home(alice,2018).

% Charlie’s principal abode is with Alice
principal_abode(charlie,alice,2018).

more_than_half_year(charlie,alice,2018).

% Alice is entitled to the §151 deduction for Charlie
entitled_to_dep_deduction(alice,charlie,2018).

/* ----------  Verification goal ---------- */
:- (  section7703b1_applies(alice,2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

```prolog
/*  ---------  Statutory rules (excerpt)  ---------  */

/*  §7703(b)(2) applies in a year Y to a taxpayer P
    if P furnishes over one-half of the cost of maintaining the household
    that is P’s home during year Y.
    We represent that factual predicate directly as
        furnishes_over_half(P,Y)
*/

sec7703b2_applies(P,Y) :-
    furnishes_over_half(P,Y).

/*  Furnishing over one-half of the cost is inferred from the statement
    that the home was “maintained by” the taxpayer for the year.           */

furnishes_over_half(P,Y) :-
    home_maintained_by(P,Y).



/*  ---------  Facts from the case  ---------  */

person(alice).
person(bob).
person(charlie).

/*  Alice has maintained the home (and therefore furnished > ½ of its cost)
    from 2017 forward.                                                      */
home_maintained_by(alice,2017).
home_maintained_by(alice,2018).
home_maintained_by(alice,2019).

/*  Alice is entitled to a §151 deduction for Charlie for 2017-2019.        */
deduction_151(alice,charlie,2017).
deduction_151(alice,charlie,2018).
deduction_151(alice,charlie,2019).

/*  Alice and Bob filed joint returns for 2017-2019.                        */
filed_joint_return(alice,bob,2017).
filed_joint_return(alice,bob,2018).
filed_joint_return(alice,bob,2019).



/*  ---------  Verification of the claim  ---------  */

:- (  sec7703b2_applies(alice,2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

Alice and Charlie live in a home maintained by Alice since September 16th, 2017. Alice is entitled to a deduction for Charlie under section 151(c) for the years 2017 to 2019. Bob lived in the same house as Alice and Charlie from September 16th, 2017 until October 10th, 2018.
question: Section 7703(b)(3) applies to Alice maintaining her home for the year 2018.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
/* ---------- Statutory rule we need ---------- */
/* Section 7703(b)(3) applies to a taxpayer for a year only if
   the taxpayer’s spouse is NOT a member of the taxpayer’s household
   at ANY time during the last 6 months of that taxable year.         */

applies_7703b3(Person,Year) :-
    spouse_of(Person,_),
    \+ spouse_member_last6months(Person,Year).

/* ---------- Helper predicates ---------- */

date_value(date(Y,M,D), Val) :-
    Val is Y*10000 + M*100 + D.

/* overlap(S1,E1,S2,E2)  –– the two closed intervals overlap */
overlap(S1,E1,S2,E2) :-
    date_value(S1,V1s), date_value(E1,V1e),
    date_value(S2,V2s), date_value(E2,V2e),
    V1s =< V2e,
    V2s =< V1e.

/* last 6-month interval of a calendar year */
last6months_start(Year, date(Year,7,1)).
last6months_end(Year,   date(Year,12,31)).

/* spouse_member_last6months(Person,Year) is true if the spouse lived
   in the household for any part of the last 6 months of the given year */
spouse_member_last6months(Person,Year) :-
    spouse_of(Person,Spouse),
    lived_in_house(Spouse,Start,End),
    last6months_start(Year,LS), last6months_end(Year,LE),
    overlap(Start,End,LS,LE).

/* ---------- Facts from the case ---------- */

spouse_of(alice,bob).
lived_in_house(bob, date(2017,9,16), date(2018,10,10)).

/* ---------- Verification goal ---------- */

:- (  applies_7703b3(alice,2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```

text: Alice and Bob got married on April 5th, 2012. Alice and Bob have a son, Charlie, who was born on September 16th, 2017. Alice and Charlie live in a home maintained by Alice since September 16th, 2017. Alice is entitled to a deduction for Charlie under section 151(c) for the years 2017 to 2019. Bob lived in the same house as Alice and Charlie from September 16th, 2017 until February 10th, 2018.
question: Section 7703(b)(3) applies to Alice maintaining her home for the year 2018.
reference_prolog:
```prolog
% ---------- Statutory rule 7703(b)(3) ----------
% 7703(b)(3) applies to an individual for a taxable year
% if, during the last 6 months of that year, the individual’s
% spouse was NOT a member of the household the individual maintains.

% Helper: convert a date(Y,M,D) structure to an integer YYYYMMDD for easy comparison.
date_num(date(Y,M,D), N) :- N is Y*10000 + M*100 + D.

% Helper: true if the two closed intervals [S1,E1] and [S2,E2] overlap.
interval_overlap(S1,E1,S2,E2) :-
    date_num(S1, N1S), date_num(E1, N1E),
    date_num(S2, N2S), date_num(E2, N2E),
    N1S =< N2E, N2S =< N1E.

% Define the “last-six-months” period for a given calendar year.
last_six_months_period(Year, date(Year,7,1), date(Year,12,31)).

% Facts: family relationships.
spouse(alice,bob).
spouse(bob,alice).

% Facts: when a person lived in Alice’s household.
% lived_in_household(Person, StartDate, EndDate).
lived_in_household(bob, date(2017,9,16), date(2018,2,10)).

% A spouse was a member of the household during the last 6 months
% of Year if the living-there interval overlaps that period.
spouse_member_last6_months(Individual, Year) :-
    spouse(Individual, Spouse),
    lived_in_household(Spouse, Start, End),
    last_six_months_period(Year, LS, LE),
    interval_overlap(Start, End, LS, LE).

% 7703(b)(3) condition holds when the spouse was NOT a member
% of the household during the last 6 months.
section_7703b3_applies(Individual, Year) :-
    \+ spouse_member_last6_months(Individual, Year).

% ---------- Query for the claim ----------
:- (  section_7703b3_applies(alice, 2018)
   -> format('Result: Entailment~n')
   ;  format('Result: Contradiction~n')
   ).
:- halt.
```
